title.zh: 卷二十七中之上 五行志
title.en: Volume 27b: Treatise on the Five Elements 2
title.raw: 卷二十七中之上 五行志 Volume 27b: Treatise on the Five Elements 2

sentences.zh: 經曰：「羞用五事。

translations.literal: Literal: The canon says: "Reverently employ the Five Affairs."
translations.idiomatic: The canon says: "Reverently employ the Five Affairs."

sentences.zh: 五事：一曰貌，二曰言，三曰視，四曰聽，五曰思。

translations.literal: Literal: The Five Affairs are: first bearing, second speech, third sight, fourth hearing, fifth thought.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Affairs are: first bearing, second speech, third sight, fourth hearing, fifth thought.

sentences.zh: 貌曰恭，言曰從，視曰明，聽曰聰，思曰睿。

translations.literal: Literal: For bearing it says reverent; for speech it says compliant; for sight it says clear; for hearing it says acute; for thought it says wise.
translations.idiomatic: For bearing it says reverent; for speech it says compliant; for sight it says clear; for hearing it says acute; for thought it says wise.

sentences.zh: 恭作肅，從作艾，明作悊，聰作謀，睿作聖。

translations.literal: Literal: Reverence produces solemnity; compliance produces order; clarity produces discernment; acuity produces deliberation; wisdom produces sageliness.
translations.idiomatic: Reverence produces solemnity; compliance produces order; clarity produces discernment; acuity produces deliberation; wisdom produces sageliness.

sentences.zh: 休徵：曰肅，時雨若；

translations.literal: Literal: A favorable sign: if solemnity is present, timely rain is in accord.
translations.idiomatic: A favorable sign: if solemnity is present, timely rain is in accord.

sentences.zh: 艾，時陽若；

translations.literal: Literal: If order is present, timely sunshine is in accord.
translations.idiomatic: If order is present, timely sunshine is in accord.

sentences.zh: 悊，時奧若；

translations.literal: Literal: If discernment is present, timely heat is in accord.
translations.idiomatic: If discernment is present, timely heat is in accord.

sentences.zh: 謀，時寒若；

translations.literal: Literal: If deliberation is present, timely cold is in accord.
translations.idiomatic: If deliberation is present, timely cold is in accord.

sentences.zh: 聖，時風若。

translations.literal: Literal: If sageliness is present, timely wind is in accord.
translations.idiomatic: If sageliness is present, timely wind is in accord.

sentences.zh: 咎徵：曰狂，恆雨若；

translations.literal: Literal: Unfavorable signs: if madness appears, there is constant rain in excess.
translations.idiomatic: Unfavorable signs: if madness appears, there is constant rain in excess.

sentences.zh: 僭，恆陽若；

translations.literal: Literal: If usurpation appears, there is constant sunshine in excess.
translations.idiomatic: If usurpation appears, there is constant sunshine in excess.

sentences.zh: 舒，恆奧若；

translations.literal: Literal: If slackness appears, there is constant heat in excess.
translations.idiomatic: If slackness appears, there is constant heat in excess.

sentences.zh: 急，恆寒若；

translations.literal: Literal: If haste appears, there is constant cold in excess.
translations.idiomatic: If haste appears, there is constant cold in excess.

sentences.zh: 霿，恆風若。」

translations.literal: Literal: If dim confusion appears, there is constant wind in excess."
translations.idiomatic: If dim confusion appears, there is constant wind in excess."

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「貌之不恭，是謂不肅，厥咎狂，厥罰恆雨，厥極惡。

translations.literal: Literal: The Tradition says: "When bearing lacks reverence, this is called lack of solemnity; its fault is madness, its punishment is constant rain, and its extreme is evil.
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "When bearing lacks reverence, this is called lack of solemnity; its fault is madness, its punishment is constant rain, and its extreme is evil.

sentences.zh: 時則有服妖，時則有龜孽，時則有雞禍，時則有下體生上之痾，時則有青眚青祥。

translations.literal: Literal: Then at times there are clothing anomalies, at times turtle prodigies, at times fowl calamities, at times illnesses where lower parts grow above, and at times green omens and green portents.
translations.idiomatic: Then at times there are clothing anomalies, at times turtle prodigies, at times fowl calamities, at times illnesses where lower parts grow above, and at times green omens and green portents.

sentences.zh: 唯金沴水。」

translations.literal: Literal: Only Metal injures Water."
translations.idiomatic: Only Metal injures Water."

sentences.zh: 說曰：凡草物之類謂之妖。

translations.literal: Literal: The explanation says: all kinds of plants and things are called anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: The explanation says: all kinds of plants and things are called anomalies.

sentences.zh: 妖猶夭胎，言尚微。

translations.literal: Literal: Anomaly is like an abnormal fetus, meaning the sign is still slight.
translations.idiomatic: Anomaly is like an abnormal fetus, meaning the sign is still slight.

sentences.zh: 蟲豸之類謂之孽。

translations.literal: Literal: Kinds of insects and creeping creatures are called prodigies.
translations.idiomatic: Kinds of insects and creeping creatures are called prodigies.

sentences.zh: 孽則牙孽矣。

translations.literal: Literal: A prodigy means a sprouting deformity.
translations.idiomatic: A prodigy means a sprouting deformity.

sentences.zh: 及六畜，謂之禍，言其著也。

translations.literal: Literal: When it reaches the six domestic animals, it is called a calamity, meaning it has become manifest.
translations.idiomatic: When it reaches the six domestic animals, it is called a calamity, meaning it has become manifest.

sentences.zh: 及人，謂之痾。

translations.literal: Literal: When it reaches humans, it is called an affliction.
translations.idiomatic: When it reaches humans, it is called an affliction.

sentences.zh: 痾，病貌，言浸深也。

translations.literal: Literal: Affliction has the appearance of illness, meaning the process is deepening gradually.
translations.idiomatic: Affliction has the appearance of illness, meaning the process is deepening gradually.

sentences.zh: 甚則異物生，謂之眚；

translations.literal: Literal: If severe, strange things are born; this is called a blight.
translations.idiomatic: If severe, strange things are born; this is called a blight.

sentences.zh: 自外來，謂之祥。

translations.literal: Literal: If it comes from outside, it is called a portent.
translations.idiomatic: If it comes from outside, it is called a portent.

sentences.zh: 祥猶禎也。

translations.literal: Literal: Portent is like an omen.
translations.idiomatic: Portent is like an omen.

sentences.zh: 氣相傷，謂之沴。

translations.literal: Literal: When qi harms one another, it is called noxious imbalance.
translations.idiomatic: When qi harms one another, it is called noxious imbalance.

sentences.zh: 沴猶臨蒞，不和意也。

translations.literal: Literal: Noxious imbalance is like oppressive presence; it means disharmony.
translations.idiomatic: Noxious imbalance is like oppressive presence; it means disharmony.

sentences.zh: 每一事云「時則」以絕之，言非必俱至，或有或亡，或在前或在後也。

translations.literal: Literal: For each item it says "then at times" to cut it off, meaning these need not all arrive together: some may appear and some not, some before and some after.
translations.idiomatic: For each item it says "then at times" to cut it off, meaning these need not all arrive together: some may appear and some not, some before and some after.

sentences.zh: 孝武時，夏侯始昌通五經，善推五行傳，以傳族子夏侯勝，下及許商，皆以教所賢弟子。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Wu's time, Xiahou Shichang mastered the Five Classics and was skilled at deriving the Five-Elements Tradition; he transmitted it to his clansman Xiahou Sheng, then down to Xu Shang, and all taught worthy disciples.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wu's time, Xiahou Shichang mastered the Five Classics and was skilled at deriving the Five-Elements Tradition; he transmitted it to his clansman Xiahou Sheng, then down to Xu Shang, and all taught worthy disciples.

sentences.zh: 其傳與劉向同，唯劉歆傳獨異。

translations.literal: Literal: That transmission matched Liu Xiang's; only Liu Xin's transmission was different.
translations.idiomatic: That transmission matched Liu Xiang's; only Liu Xin's transmission was different.

sentences.zh: 貌之不恭，是謂不肅。

translations.literal: Literal: When bearing is not reverent, this is called lacking solemnity.
translations.idiomatic: When bearing is not reverent, this is called lacking solemnity.

sentences.zh: 肅，敬也。

translations.literal: Literal: Solemnity means respect.
translations.idiomatic: Solemnity means respect.

sentences.zh: 內曰恭，外曰敬。

translations.literal: Literal: Inwardly it is called reverence; outwardly it is called respect.
translations.idiomatic: Inwardly it is called reverence; outwardly it is called respect.

sentences.zh: 人君行己，體貌不恭，怠慢驕蹇，則不能敬萬事，失在狂易，故其咎狂也。

translations.literal: Literal: If a ruler's personal conduct and bearing are irreverent, negligent, arrogant, and overbearing, then he cannot respect the myriad affairs; the fault lies in frenzy and caprice, so its blame is madness.
translations.idiomatic: If a ruler's personal conduct and bearing are irreverent, negligent, arrogant, and overbearing, then he cannot respect the myriad affairs; the fault lies in frenzy and caprice, so its blame is madness.

sentences.zh: 上嫚下暴，則陰氣勝，故其罰常雨也。

translations.literal: Literal: If superiors are contemptuous and inferiors violent, yin qi prevails; thus the punishment is constant rain.
translations.idiomatic: If superiors are contemptuous and inferiors violent, yin qi prevails; thus the punishment is constant rain.

sentences.zh: 水傷百穀，衣食不足，則姦軌並作，故其極惡也。

translations.literal: Literal: When water harms the hundred grains and clothing and food are insufficient, then treachery and disorder arise together; thus the extreme is evil.
translations.idiomatic: When water harms the hundred grains and clothing and food are insufficient, then treachery and disorder arise together; thus the extreme is evil.

sentences.zh: 一曰，民多被刑，或形貌醜惡，亦是也。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says: when many people are punished, or their forms and features become ugly, this also belongs here.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: when many people are punished, or their forms and features become ugly, this also belongs here.

sentences.zh: 風俗狂慢，變節易度，則為剽輕奇怪之服，故有服妖。

translations.literal: Literal: When customs are wild and insolent, changing standards and measures, then there are frivolous, bizarre garments; therefore there are clothing anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: When customs are wild and insolent, changing standards and measures, then there are frivolous, bizarre garments; therefore there are clothing anomalies.

sentences.zh: 水類動，故有龜孽。

translations.literal: Literal: The water category is prone to movement; therefore there are turtle prodigies.
translations.idiomatic: The water category is prone to movement; therefore there are turtle prodigies.

sentences.zh: 於易，巽為雞，雞有冠距文武之貌。

translations.literal: Literal: In the Changes, Xun is fowl; the fowl has crest and spur, the signs of civil and martial bearing.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, Xun is fowl; the fowl has crest and spur, the signs of civil and martial bearing.

sentences.zh: 不為威儀，貌氣毀，故有雞禍。

translations.literal: Literal: When these do not serve dignified deportment and the qi of bearing is damaged, there are fowl calamities.
translations.idiomatic: When these do not serve dignified deportment and the qi of bearing is damaged, there are fowl calamities.

sentences.zh: 一曰，水歲雞多死及為怪，亦是也。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says: in water years, many fowl die or become strange; this is also of this type.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: in water years, many fowl die or become strange; this is also of this type.

sentences.zh: 上失威儀，則下有彊臣害君上者，故有下體生於上之痾。

translations.literal: Literal: When those above lose dignity, then below there are powerful ministers who harm the ruler; therefore there are afflictions of lower parts growing above.
translations.idiomatic: When those above lose dignity, then below there are powerful ministers who harm the ruler; therefore there are afflictions of lower parts growing above.

sentences.zh: 木色青，故有青眚青祥。

translations.literal: Literal: Wood's color is green; therefore there are green blights and green portents.
translations.idiomatic: Wood's color is green; therefore there are green blights and green portents.

sentences.zh: 凡貌傷者病木氣，木氣病則金沴之，衝氣相通也。

translations.literal: Literal: In all cases where bearing is damaged, wood qi is ill; when wood qi is ill, metal injures it, as opposing qi communicate.
translations.idiomatic: In all cases where bearing is damaged, wood qi is ill; when wood qi is ill, metal injures it, as opposing qi communicate.

sentences.zh: 於易，震在東方，為春為木也；

translations.literal: Literal: In the Changes, Zhen is in the east, and is spring and wood;
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, Zhen is in the east, and is spring and wood;

sentences.zh: 兌在西方，為秋為金也；

translations.literal: Literal: Dui is in the west, and is autumn and metal;
translations.idiomatic: Dui is in the west, and is autumn and metal;

sentences.zh: 離在南方，為夏為火也；

translations.literal: Literal: Li is in the south, and is summer and fire;
translations.idiomatic: Li is in the south, and is summer and fire;

sentences.zh: 坎在北方，為冬為水也。

translations.literal: Literal: Kan is in the north, and is winter and water.
translations.idiomatic: Kan is in the north, and is winter and water.

sentences.zh: 春與秋，日夜分，寒暑平，是以金木之氣易以相變，故貌傷則致秋陰常雨，言傷則致春陽常旱也。

translations.literal: Literal: Spring and autumn divide day and night and balance cold and heat, so metal and wood qi can readily transform into one another; therefore damage to bearing brings persistent autumn yin and constant rain, while damage to speech brings persistent spring yang and constant drought.
translations.idiomatic: Spring and autumn divide day and night and balance cold and heat, so metal and wood qi can readily transform into one another; therefore damage to bearing brings persistent autumn yin and constant rain, while damage to speech brings persistent spring yang and constant drought.

sentences.zh: 至於冬夏，日夜相反，寒暑殊絕，水火之氣不得相併，故視傷常奧，聽傷常寒者，其氣然也。

translations.literal: Literal: As for winter and summer, day and night oppose each other and cold and heat are utterly distinct, so water and fire qi cannot merge; therefore damage to sight brings constant heat and damage to hearing brings constant cold: this is due to their qi.
translations.idiomatic: As for winter and summer, day and night oppose each other and cold and heat are utterly distinct, so water and fire qi cannot merge; therefore damage to sight brings constant heat and damage to hearing brings constant cold: this is due to their qi.

sentences.zh: 逆之，其極曰惡；

translations.literal: Literal: If one goes against it, the extreme is called evil;
translations.idiomatic: If one goes against it, the extreme is called evil;

sentences.zh: 順之，其福曰攸好德。

translations.literal: Literal: If one follows it, the blessing is called loving virtue.
translations.idiomatic: If one follows it, the blessing is called loving virtue.

sentences.zh: 劉歆貌傳曰有鱗蟲之孽，羊禍，鼻痾。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin's tradition on bearing says there are scale-creature prodigies, sheep calamities, and nose afflictions.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin's tradition on bearing says there are scale-creature prodigies, sheep calamities, and nose afflictions.

sentences.zh: 說以為於天文東方辰為龍星，故為鱗蟲；

translations.literal: Literal: His explanation says that in celestial patterns the eastern lodges are dragon stars, therefore scale-creatures;
translations.idiomatic: His explanation says that in celestial patterns the eastern lodges are dragon stars, therefore scale-creatures;

sentences.zh: 於易兌為羊，木為金所病，故致羊禍，與常雨同應。

translations.literal: Literal: and in the Changes, Dui is sheep; wood is harmed by metal, therefore sheep calamity occurs, corresponding with constant rain.
translations.idiomatic: and in the Changes, Dui is sheep; wood is harmed by metal, therefore sheep calamity occurs, corresponding with constant rain.

sentences.zh: 此說非是。

translations.literal: Literal: This explanation is incorrect.
translations.idiomatic: This explanation is incorrect.

sentences.zh: 春與秋，氣陰陽相敵，木病金盛，故能相并，唯此一事耳。

translations.literal: Literal: Only in spring and autumn do yin and yang contend in qi, and when wood is diseased and metal strong they can merge; it is only this one case.
translations.idiomatic: Only in spring and autumn do yin and yang contend in qi, and when wood is diseased and metal strong they can merge; it is only this one case.

sentences.zh: 禍與妖痾祥眚同類，不得獨異。

translations.literal: Literal: Calamities, anomalies, afflictions, portents, and blights are of one kind and cannot alone be made different.
translations.idiomatic: Calamities, anomalies, afflictions, portents, and blights are of one kind and cannot alone be made different.

sentences.zh: 史記成公十六年，公會諸侯于周，單襄公見晉厲公視遠步高，告公曰：「晉將有亂。」

translations.literal: Literal: In the Records, year sixteen of Duke Cheng: the duke met the feudal lords at Zhou; Shan Xianggong saw Duke Li of Jin looking far and stepping high, and told the duke: "Jin will have disorder."
translations.idiomatic: In the Records, year sixteen of Duke Cheng: the duke met the feudal lords at Zhou; Shan Xianggong saw Duke Li of Jin looking far and stepping high, and told the duke: "Jin will have disorder."

sentences.zh: 魯侯曰：「敢問天道也？

translations.literal: Literal: The marquis of Lu said: "May I ask, is this the Way of Heaven,
translations.idiomatic: The marquis of Lu said: "May I ask, is this the Way of Heaven,

sentences.zh: 抑人故也？」

translations.literal: Literal: or a human cause?"
translations.idiomatic: or a human cause?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「吾非瞽史，焉知天道？

translations.literal: Literal: He answered: "I am not a blind historian; how would I know Heaven's Way?
translations.idiomatic: He answered: "I am not a blind historian; how would I know Heaven's Way?

sentences.zh: 吾見晉君之容，殆必禍者也。

translations.literal: Literal: I see the bearing of the ruler of Jin; he is close to disaster for certain.
translations.idiomatic: I see the bearing of the ruler of Jin; he is close to disaster for certain.

sentences.zh: 夫君子目以定體，足以從之，是以觀其容而知其心矣。

translations.literal: Literal: For a gentleman, the eyes settle the body and the feet follow them; thus by observing bearing one knows the heart.
translations.idiomatic: For a gentleman, the eyes settle the body and the feet follow them; thus by observing bearing one knows the heart.

sentences.zh: 目以處誼，足以步目。

translations.literal: Literal: The eyes set what is proper, and the feet step by the eyes.
translations.idiomatic: The eyes set what is proper, and the feet step by the eyes.

sentences.zh: 晉侯視遠而足高，目不在體，而足不步目，其心必異矣。

translations.literal: Literal: The marquis of Jin looks far while his feet rise high: the eyes are not on the body, and the feet do not follow the eyes; his mind must be deviant.
translations.idiomatic: The marquis of Jin looks far while his feet rise high: the eyes are not on the body, and the feet do not follow the eyes; his mind must be deviant.

sentences.zh: 目體不相從，何以能久？

translations.literal: Literal: If eye and body do not accord, how can it last long?
translations.idiomatic: If eye and body do not accord, how can it last long?

sentences.zh: 夫合諸侯，民之大事也，於是虖觀存亡。

translations.literal: Literal: Gathering the feudal lords is a great matter of the people; from this one observes survival or ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Gathering the feudal lords is a great matter of the people; from this one observes survival or ruin.

sentences.zh: 故國將無咎，其君在會，步言視聽必皆無謫，則可以知德矣。

translations.literal: Literal: So when a state is about to be blameless, its ruler at such meetings will have steps, speech, sight, and hearing all without fault, and from that virtue may be known.
translations.idiomatic: So when a state is about to be blameless, its ruler at such meetings will have steps, speech, sight, and hearing all without fault, and from that virtue may be known.

sentences.zh: 視遠，曰絕其誼；

translations.literal: Literal: Looking far means cutting off propriety;
translations.idiomatic: Looking far means cutting off propriety;

sentences.zh: 足高，曰棄其德；

translations.literal: Literal: raising the feet high means abandoning virtue;
translations.idiomatic: raising the feet high means abandoning virtue;

sentences.zh: 言爽，曰反其信；

translations.literal: Literal: errant speech means betraying trust;
translations.idiomatic: errant speech means betraying trust;

sentences.zh: 聽淫，曰離其名。

translations.literal: Literal: licentious hearing means departing from reputation.
translations.idiomatic: licentious hearing means departing from reputation.

sentences.zh: 夫目以處誼，足以踐德，口以庇信，耳以聽名者也，故不可不慎。

translations.literal: Literal: Eyes are for placing propriety, feet for treading virtue, mouth for preserving trust, ears for hearing reputation; therefore one cannot but be cautious.
translations.idiomatic: Eyes are for placing propriety, feet for treading virtue, mouth for preserving trust, ears for hearing reputation; therefore one cannot but be cautious.

sentences.zh: 偏喪有咎；

translations.literal: Literal: If one part is lost, there is blame;
translations.idiomatic: If one part is lost, there is blame;

sentences.zh: 既喪，則國從之。

translations.literal: Literal: once lost, the state follows it.
translations.idiomatic: once lost, the state follows it.

sentences.zh: 晉侯爽二，吾是以云。」

translations.literal: Literal: The marquis of Jin has missed two; that is why I say so."
translations.idiomatic: The marquis of Jin has missed two; that is why I say so."

sentences.zh: 後二年，晉人殺厲公。

translations.literal: Literal: Two years later, men of Jin killed Duke Li.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, men of Jin killed Duke Li.

sentences.zh: 凡此屬，皆貌不恭之咎云。

translations.literal: Literal: All such cases are said to be blame for irreverent bearing.
translations.idiomatic: All such cases are said to be blame for irreverent bearing.

sentences.zh: 左氏使桓公十三年，楚屈瑕伐羅，鬥伯比送之，還謂其馭曰：「莫囂必敗，舉止高，心不固矣。」

translations.literal: Literal: In the Zuo Tradition, year thirteen of Duke Huan, Qu Xia of Chu attacked Luo. Dou Bobo saw him off and on returning told his charioteer: "Mo Ao will surely be defeated; his bearing is haughty and his mind is not steady."
translations.idiomatic: In the Zuo Tradition, year thirteen of Duke Huan, Qu Xia of Chu attacked Luo. Dou Bobo saw him off and on returning told his charioteer: "Mo Ao will surely be defeated; his bearing is haughty and his mind is not steady."

sentences.zh: 遽見楚子以告。

translations.literal: Literal: He quickly saw the king of Chu and reported it.
translations.idiomatic: He quickly saw the king of Chu and reported it.

sentences.zh: 楚子使賴人追之，弗及。

translations.literal: Literal: The king of Chu sent men of Lai to pursue him, but they did not catch up.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Chu sent men of Lai to pursue him, but they did not catch up.

sentences.zh: 莫囂行，遂無次，且不設備。

translations.literal: Literal: As Mo Ao marched, he made no proper encampment and made no preparations.
translations.idiomatic: As Mo Ao marched, he made no proper encampment and made no preparations.

sentences.zh: 及羅，羅人軍之，大敗。

translations.literal: Literal: When they reached Luo, the men of Luo engaged them and inflicted a great defeat.
translations.idiomatic: When they reached Luo, the men of Luo engaged them and inflicted a great defeat.

sentences.zh: 莫囂縊死。

translations.literal: Literal: Mo Ao hanged himself to death.
translations.idiomatic: Mo Ao hanged himself to death.

sentences.zh: 釐公十一年，周使內史過賜晉惠公命，受玉，惰。

translations.literal: Literal: In year eleven of Duke Xi, Zhou sent Internal Historian Guo to bestow the royal charge on Duke Hui of Jin; when receiving the jade, he was indolent.
translations.idiomatic: In year eleven of Duke Xi, Zhou sent Internal Historian Guo to bestow the royal charge on Duke Hui of Jin; when receiving the jade, he was indolent.

sentences.zh: 過歸告王曰：「晉侯其無後乎！

translations.literal: Literal: Guo returned and told the king: "Will the marquis of Jin have no descendants?
translations.idiomatic: Guo returned and told the king: "Will the marquis of Jin have no descendants?

sentences.zh: 王賜之命，而惰於受瑞，先自棄也已，其何繼之有！

translations.literal: Literal: The king bestows his charge, yet he is negligent in receiving the token; he has cast himself off first, so what succession can he have?
translations.idiomatic: The king bestows his charge, yet he is negligent in receiving the token; he has cast himself off first, so what succession can he have?

sentences.zh: 禮，國之幹也；

translations.literal: Literal: Ritual is the trunk of a state;
translations.idiomatic: Ritual is the trunk of a state;

sentences.zh: 敬，禮之輿也。

translations.literal: Literal: reverence is the carriage of ritual.
translations.idiomatic: reverence is the carriage of ritual.

sentences.zh: 不敬則禮不行，禮不行則上下昏，何以長世！」

translations.literal: Literal: Without reverence, ritual does not operate; if ritual does not operate, above and below are darkened. How could such a house endure long?"
translations.idiomatic: Without reverence, ritual does not operate; if ritual does not operate, above and below are darkened. How could such a house endure long?"

sentences.zh: 二十一年，晉惠公卒，子懷公立，晉人殺之，更立文公。

translations.literal: Literal: In year twenty-one Duke Hui of Jin died; his son Duke Huai was installed, but the Jin people killed him and installed Duke Wen instead.
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-one Duke Hui of Jin died; his son Duke Huai was installed, but the Jin people killed him and installed Duke Wen instead.

sentences.zh: 成公十三年，晉侯使郤錡乞師于魯，將事不敬。

translations.literal: Literal: In year thirteen of Duke Cheng, the marquis of Jin sent Xi Qi to request troops from Lu; in conducting the mission he was disrespectful.
translations.idiomatic: In year thirteen of Duke Cheng, the marquis of Jin sent Xi Qi to request troops from Lu; in conducting the mission he was disrespectful.

sentences.zh: 孟獻子曰：「郤氏其亡乎！

translations.literal: Literal: Meng Xianzi said: "Will the Xi clan perish?
translations.idiomatic: Meng Xianzi said: "Will the Xi clan perish?

sentences.zh: 禮，身之幹也；

translations.literal: Literal: Ritual is the trunk of the person;
translations.idiomatic: Ritual is the trunk of the person;

sentences.zh: 敬，身之基也。

translations.literal: Literal: reverence is the foundation of the person.
translations.idiomatic: reverence is the foundation of the person.

sentences.zh: 郤子無基。

translations.literal: Literal: Master Xi has no foundation.
translations.idiomatic: Master Xi has no foundation.

sentences.zh: 且先君之嗣卿也，受命以求師，將社稷是衛，而惰棄君命也，不亡何為！」

translations.literal: Literal: Moreover, he was the hereditary minister of former rulers, receiving command to seek allied troops to protect the altars of state, yet he was negligent and cast aside his ruler's command; if he does not perish, what would?
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, he was the hereditary minister of former rulers, receiving command to seek allied troops to protect the altars of state, yet he was negligent and cast aside his ruler's command; if he does not perish, what would?

sentences.zh: 十七年，郤氏亡。

translations.literal: Literal: In year seventeen, the Xi clan perished.
translations.idiomatic: In year seventeen, the Xi clan perished.

sentences.zh: 成公十三年，諸侯朝王，遂從劉康公伐秦。

translations.literal: Literal: In year thirteen of Duke Cheng, the feudal lords paid court to the king, then followed Lord Liu Kang in attacking Qin.
translations.idiomatic: In year thirteen of Duke Cheng, the feudal lords paid court to the king, then followed Lord Liu Kang in attacking Qin.

sentences.zh: 成肅公受賑于社，不敬。

translations.literal: Literal: Lord Cheng Su accepted the sacrificial meat at the altar and was disrespectful.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Cheng Su accepted the sacrificial meat at the altar and was disrespectful.

sentences.zh: 劉子曰：「吾聞之曰，民受天地之中以生，所謂命也。

translations.literal: Literal: Lord Liu said: "I have heard that the people receive life from the balanced qi of Heaven and Earth; this is what is called mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Liu said: "I have heard that the people receive life from the balanced qi of Heaven and Earth; this is what is called mandate.

sentences.zh: 是以有禮義動作威儀之則，以定命也。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore there are rules of ritual, righteousness, movement, and dignified bearing to settle that mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore there are rules of ritual, righteousness, movement, and dignified bearing to settle that mandate.

sentences.zh: 能者養以之福，不能者敗以取禍，是故君子勤禮，小人盡力。

translations.literal: Literal: Those able nourish it to gain fortune; those unable spoil it and incur disaster. Thus gentlemen diligently observe ritual, while lesser people exert all their strength.
translations.idiomatic: Those able nourish it to gain fortune; those unable spoil it and incur disaster. Thus gentlemen diligently observe ritual, while lesser people exert all their strength.

sentences.zh: 勤禮莫如致敬，盡力莫如惇篤。

translations.literal: Literal: In diligence to ritual, nothing equals utmost reverence; in full exertion, nothing equals sincerity and steadfastness.
translations.idiomatic: In diligence to ritual, nothing equals utmost reverence; in full exertion, nothing equals sincerity and steadfastness.

sentences.zh: 敬在養神，篤在守業。

translations.literal: Literal: Reverence lies in nourishing the spirit; steadfastness lies in guarding one's charge.
translations.idiomatic: Reverence lies in nourishing the spirit; steadfastness lies in guarding one's charge.

sentences.zh: 國之大事，在祀與戎。

translations.literal: Literal: A state's great affairs lie in sacrifice and war.
translations.idiomatic: A state's great affairs lie in sacrifice and war.

sentences.zh: 祀有執膰，戎有受賑，神之大節也。

translations.literal: Literal: In sacrifice there is receiving sacrificial flesh; in war there is receiving military provisions. These are great ritual nodes of the spirits.
translations.idiomatic: In sacrifice there is receiving sacrificial flesh; in war there is receiving military provisions. These are great ritual nodes of the spirits.

sentences.zh: 今成子惰，棄其命矣，其不反虖！」

translations.literal: Literal: Now Chengzi is negligent and has cast away his mandate. How could he return safely?"
translations.idiomatic: Now Chengzi is negligent and has cast away his mandate. How could he return safely?"

sentences.zh: 五月，成肅公卒。

translations.literal: Literal: In the fifth month, Lord Cheng Su died.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month, Lord Cheng Su died.

sentences.zh: 成公十四年，衛定公享苦成叔，甯惠子相。

translations.literal: Literal: In year fourteen of Duke Cheng, Duke Ding of Wei hosted Ku Chengshu, with Ning Huizi as master of ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: In year fourteen of Duke Cheng, Duke Ding of Wei hosted Ku Chengshu, with Ning Huizi as master of ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 苦成叔敖，甯子曰：「苦成家其亡虖！

translations.literal: Literal: Ku Chengshu was arrogant. Ningzi said: "Will the Ku Cheng house perish?
translations.idiomatic: Ku Chengshu was arrogant. Ningzi said: "Will the Ku Cheng house perish?

sentences.zh: 古之為享食也，以觀威儀省禍福也。

translations.literal: Literal: In antiquity banquets were held to observe dignified bearing and examine fortune and disaster.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity banquets were held to observe dignified bearing and examine fortune and disaster.

sentences.zh: 故《詩》曰：『兕觥其觩，旨酒思柔，匪儌匪傲，萬福來求。』

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore the Odes says: "The rhinoceros-horn cup curves well; fine wine should be gentle in intent; neither presumptuous nor proud, and ten thousand blessings come seeking."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore the Odes says: "The rhinoceros-horn cup curves well; fine wine should be gentle in intent; neither presumptuous nor proud, and ten thousand blessings come seeking."

sentences.zh: 今夫子傲，取禍之道也。」

translations.literal: Literal: Now this gentleman is arrogant; this is the path of courting disaster."
translations.idiomatic: Now this gentleman is arrogant; this is the path of courting disaster."

sentences.zh: 後三年，苦成家亡。

translations.literal: Literal: Three years later, the Ku Cheng house perished.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later, the Ku Cheng house perished.

sentences.zh: 襄公七年，衛孫文子聘于魯，君登亦登。

translations.literal: Literal: In year seven of Duke Xiang, Sun Wenzi of Wei visited Lu; when the ruler mounted, he too mounted.
translations.idiomatic: In year seven of Duke Xiang, Sun Wenzi of Wei visited Lu; when the ruler mounted, he too mounted.

sentences.zh: 叔孫穆子相，趨進曰：「諸侯之會，寡君未嘗後衛君，今吾子不後寡君，寡君未知所過，吾子其少安！」

translations.literal: Literal: Shusun Muzi, as ceremony master, advanced quickly and said: "At gatherings of feudal lords, my ruler has never lagged behind the ruler of Wei. Now you do not fall behind my ruler. My ruler does not know his fault; please settle yourself a little."
translations.idiomatic: Shusun Muzi, as ceremony master, advanced quickly and said: "At gatherings of feudal lords, my ruler has never lagged behind the ruler of Wei. Now you do not fall behind my ruler. My ruler does not know his fault; please settle yourself a little."

sentences.zh: 孫子亡辭，亦亡悛容。

translations.literal: Literal: Sunzi had no reply and showed no change in expression.
translations.idiomatic: Sunzi had no reply and showed no change in expression.

sentences.zh: 穆子曰：「孫子必亡。

translations.literal: Literal: Muzi said: "Sunzi will surely perish.
translations.idiomatic: Muzi said: "Sunzi will surely perish.

sentences.zh: 為臣而君，過而不悛，亡之本也。」

translations.literal: Literal: Being a minister yet acting as ruler, and erring without reform, is the root of ruin."
translations.idiomatic: Being a minister yet acting as ruler, and erring without reform, is the root of ruin."

sentences.zh: 十四年，孫子逐其君而外叛。

translations.literal: Literal: In year fourteen, Sunzi expelled his ruler and rebelled abroad.
translations.idiomatic: In year fourteen, Sunzi expelled his ruler and rebelled abroad.

sentences.zh: 襄公二十八年，蔡景侯歸自晉，入于鄭。

translations.literal: Literal: In year twenty-eight of Duke Xiang, Marquis Jing of Cai returned from Jin and entered Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-eight of Duke Xiang, Marquis Jing of Cai returned from Jin and entered Zheng.

sentences.zh: 鄭伯享之，不敬。

translations.literal: Literal: The earl of Zheng hosted him, and he was disrespectful.
translations.idiomatic: The earl of Zheng hosted him, and he was disrespectful.

sentences.zh: 子產曰：「蔡君其不免虖！

translations.literal: Literal: Zichan said: "Will the ruler of Cai not escape death?
translations.idiomatic: Zichan said: "Will the ruler of Cai not escape death?

sentences.zh: 日其過此也，君使子展往勞于東門，而敖。

translations.literal: Literal: On the day he passed here, our ruler sent Zizhan to comfort him at the east gate, yet he was arrogant.
translations.idiomatic: On the day he passed here, our ruler sent Zizhan to comfort him at the east gate, yet he was arrogant.

sentences.zh: 吾曰：『猶將更之。』

translations.literal: Literal: I said, "He may still change."
translations.idiomatic: I said, "He may still change."

sentences.zh: 今還，受享而惰，乃其心也。

translations.literal: Literal: Now on returning, he receives a banquet and is still negligent: this is his heart.
translations.idiomatic: Now on returning, he receives a banquet and is still negligent: this is his heart.

sentences.zh: 君小國，事大國，而惰敖以為己心，將得死虖？

translations.literal: Literal: His is a small state serving great states, yet he makes negligence and arrogance his settled mind. How can he avoid death?
translations.idiomatic: His is a small state serving great states, yet he makes negligence and arrogance his settled mind. How can he avoid death?

sentences.zh: 君若不免，必由其子。

translations.literal: Literal: If the ruler does not escape, it will surely be through his son.
translations.idiomatic: If the ruler does not escape, it will surely be through his son.

sentences.zh: 淫而不父，如是者必有子禍。」

translations.literal: Literal: One who is licentious and not fatherly must suffer calamity through a son."
translations.idiomatic: One who is licentious and not fatherly must suffer calamity through a son."

sentences.zh: 三十年，為世子般所殺。

translations.literal: Literal: In year thirty, he was killed by the heir apparent Ban.
translations.idiomatic: In year thirty, he was killed by the heir apparent Ban.

sentences.zh: 襄公三十一年，公薨。

translations.literal: Literal: In year thirty-one of Duke Xiang, the duke died.
translations.idiomatic: In year thirty-one of Duke Xiang, the duke died.

sentences.zh: 季武子將立公子裯，穆叔曰：「是人也，居喪而不哀，在慼而有嘉容，是謂不度。

translations.literal: Literal: Ji Wuzi wanted to install Prince Chou. Mushu said: "This man, while in mourning, is not sorrowful; in grief, he wears a pleased expression. This is called lacking measure.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Wuzi wanted to install Prince Chou. Mushu said: "This man, while in mourning, is not sorrowful; in grief, he wears a pleased expression. This is called lacking measure.

sentences.zh: 不度之人，鮮不為患，若果立，必為季氏憂。」

translations.literal: Literal: Such people seldom fail to bring trouble. If he is installed, he will surely cause worry for the Ji clan."
translations.idiomatic: Such people seldom fail to bring trouble. If he is installed, he will surely cause worry for the Ji clan."

sentences.zh: 武子弗聽，卒立之。

translations.literal: Literal: Wuzi did not listen and in the end installed him.
translations.idiomatic: Wuzi did not listen and in the end installed him.

sentences.zh: 比及葬，三易衰，衰衽如故衰。

translations.literal: Literal: Before burial, he changed mourning garb three times, and the hem of his mourning dress was like ordinary clothes.
translations.idiomatic: Before burial, he changed mourning garb three times, and the hem of his mourning dress was like ordinary clothes.

sentences.zh: 是為昭公。

translations.literal: Literal: This became Duke Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: This became Duke Zhao.

sentences.zh: 立二十五年，聽讒攻季氏。

translations.literal: Literal: After twenty-five years on the throne, he listened to slander and attacked the Ji clan.
translations.idiomatic: After twenty-five years on the throne, he listened to slander and attacked the Ji clan.

sentences.zh: 兵敗，出奔，死于外。

translations.literal: Literal: His army was defeated; he fled into exile and died abroad.
translations.idiomatic: His army was defeated; he fled into exile and died abroad.

sentences.zh: 襄公三十一年，衛北宮文子見楚令尹圍之儀，言於衛侯曰：「令尹似君矣，將有它志；

translations.literal: Literal: In year thirty-one of Duke Xiang, Beigong Wenzi of Wei observed the bearing of Wei, commandant of Chu, and said to the marquis of Wei: "The commandant acts like a ruler; he harbors another ambition.
translations.idiomatic: In year thirty-one of Duke Xiang, Beigong Wenzi of Wei observed the bearing of Wei, commandant of Chu, and said to the marquis of Wei: "The commandant acts like a ruler; he harbors another ambition.

sentences.zh: 雖獲其志，弗能終也。」

translations.literal: Literal: Even if he attains his aim, he will not be able to finish well."
translations.idiomatic: Even if he attains his aim, he will not be able to finish well."

sentences.zh: 公曰：「子何以知之？」

translations.literal: Literal: The duke said: "How do you know this?"
translations.idiomatic: The duke said: "How do you know this?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「《詩》云『敬慎威儀，惟民之則』，令尹無威儀，民無則焉。

translations.literal: Literal: He replied: "The Odes says, "Reverent and cautious in dignified bearing, thereby a model for the people." The commandant has no dignified bearing, so the people have no model.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "The Odes says, "Reverent and cautious in dignified bearing, thereby a model for the people." The commandant has no dignified bearing, so the people have no model.

sentences.zh: 民所不則，以在民上，不可以終。

translations.literal: Literal: What the people cannot model cannot remain long while set above them."
translations.idiomatic: What the people cannot model cannot remain long while set above them."

sentences.zh: 昭公十一年夏，周單子會於戚，視下言徐。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer of Duke Zhao year eleven, Lord Shan of Zhou met at Qi; his gaze was downward and his speech slow.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Duke Zhao year eleven, Lord Shan of Zhou met at Qi; his gaze was downward and his speech slow.

sentences.zh: 晉叔向曰：「單子其死虖！

translations.literal: Literal: Shu Xiang of Jin said: "Will Lord Shan die soon?
translations.idiomatic: Shu Xiang of Jin said: "Will Lord Shan die soon?

sentences.zh: 朝有著定，會有表，衣有襘，帶有結。

translations.literal: Literal: At court there are fixed stations; in assemblies there are markers; on robes there are clasps; on belts there are knots.
translations.idiomatic: At court there are fixed stations; in assemblies there are markers; on robes there are clasps; on belts there are knots.

sentences.zh: 會朝之言必聞于表著之位，所以昭事序也；

translations.literal: Literal: Words at assemblies and court must be heard at the marked and assigned positions, so affairs and rank are made clear.
translations.idiomatic: Words at assemblies and court must be heard at the marked and assigned positions, so affairs and rank are made clear.

sentences.zh: 視不過結襘之中，所以道容貌也。

translations.literal: Literal: One's gaze should not pass beyond the space between belt-knot and clasp, so as to guide bearing.
translations.idiomatic: One's gaze should not pass beyond the space between belt-knot and clasp, so as to guide bearing.

sentences.zh: 言以命之，容貌以明之，失則有闕。

translations.literal: Literal: Speech gives command and bearing makes it clear; if lost, there is defect.
translations.idiomatic: Speech gives command and bearing makes it clear; if lost, there is defect.

sentences.zh: 今單子為王官伯，而命事於會，視不登帶，言不過步，貌不道容而言不昭矣。

translations.literal: Literal: Now Lord Shan is the royal minister elder and gives commands at the assembly, yet his gaze does not rise to his belt and his words do not pass a few steps; his bearing does not guide appearance and his speech lacks clarity.
translations.idiomatic: Now Lord Shan is the royal minister elder and gives commands at the assembly, yet his gaze does not rise to his belt and his words do not pass a few steps; his bearing does not guide appearance and his speech lacks clarity.

sentences.zh: 不道不恭，不昭不從，無守氣矣。」

translations.literal: Literal: Not guiding means irreverent; not clear means noncompliant; he has no preserving qi."
translations.idiomatic: Not guiding means irreverent; not clear means noncompliant; he has no preserving qi."

sentences.zh: 十二月，單成公卒。

translations.literal: Literal: In the twelfth month, Lord Shan Cheng died.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month, Lord Shan Cheng died.

sentences.zh: 晉魏舒合諸侯之大夫于翟泉，將以城成周。

translations.literal: Literal: Jin's Wei Shu gathered the feudal ministers at Diquan and was about to wall Chengzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Jin's Wei Shu gathered the feudal ministers at Diquan and was about to wall Chengzhou.

sentences.zh: 魏子蒞政，衛彪傒曰：「將建天子，而易位以令，非誼也。

translations.literal: Literal: As Wei governed affairs, Biao Xi of Wei said: "To establish the Son of Heaven yet alter ranks to issue commands is not right.
translations.idiomatic: As Wei governed affairs, Biao Xi of Wei said: "To establish the Son of Heaven yet alter ranks to issue commands is not right.

sentences.zh: 大事奸誼，必有大咎。

translations.literal: Literal: When a great affair violates rightness, there must be great blame.
translations.idiomatic: When a great affair violates rightness, there must be great blame.

sentences.zh: 晉不失諸侯，魏子其不免虖！」

translations.literal: Literal: If Jin does not lose the feudal lords, Wei will likely not escape."
translations.idiomatic: If Jin does not lose the feudal lords, Wei will likely not escape."

sentences.zh: 是行也，魏獻子屬役於韓簡子，而田於大陸，焚焉而死。

translations.literal: Literal: On this campaign Wei Xianzi assigned labor duty to Han Jianzi, then hunted on Dalu and was burned to death there.
translations.idiomatic: On this campaign Wei Xianzi assigned labor duty to Han Jianzi, then hunted on Dalu and was burned to death there.

sentences.zh: 定公十五年，邾隱公朝於魯，執玉高，其容仰。

translations.literal: Literal: In year fifteen of Duke Ding, Duke Yin of Zhu paid court in Lu, holding the jade high with his bearing tilted upward.
translations.idiomatic: In year fifteen of Duke Ding, Duke Yin of Zhu paid court in Lu, holding the jade high with his bearing tilted upward.

sentences.zh: 公受玉卑，其容俯。

translations.literal: Literal: The duke received the jade low, his bearing bowed downward.
translations.idiomatic: The duke received the jade low, his bearing bowed downward.

sentences.zh: 子贛觀焉，曰：「以禮觀之，二君者皆有死亡焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Zigong observed and said: "Judging by ritual, both these rulers face death and downfall.
translations.idiomatic: Zigong observed and said: "Judging by ritual, both these rulers face death and downfall.

sentences.zh: 夫禮，死生存亡之體也。

translations.literal: Literal: Ritual is the frame of life and death, survival and ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual is the frame of life and death, survival and ruin.

sentences.zh: 將左右周旋，進退俯仰，於是虖取之；

translations.literal: Literal: Its turns to left and right, its circling movement, its advancing, retreating, bowing, and lifting are all taken from it.
translations.idiomatic: Its turns to left and right, its circling movement, its advancing, retreating, bowing, and lifting are all taken from it.

sentences.zh: 朝祀喪戎，於是虖觀之。

translations.literal: Literal: Court audiences, sacrifices, mourning, and war are observed through it.
translations.idiomatic: Court audiences, sacrifices, mourning, and war are observed through it.

sentences.zh: 今正月相朝，而皆不度，心已亡矣。

translations.literal: Literal: Now in the first month they court one another, yet both are out of measure; their hearts are already lost.
translations.idiomatic: Now in the first month they court one another, yet both are out of measure; their hearts are already lost.

sentences.zh: 嘉事不體，何以能久？

translations.literal: Literal: If auspicious affairs do not fit the proper form, how can they endure long?
translations.idiomatic: If auspicious affairs do not fit the proper form, how can they endure long?

sentences.zh: 高仰，驕也；

translations.literal: Literal: Holding high and looking up is arrogance;
translations.idiomatic: Holding high and looking up is arrogance;

sentences.zh: 卑俯，替也。

translations.literal: Literal: holding low and bowing down is decline.
translations.idiomatic: holding low and bowing down is decline.

sentences.zh: 驕近亂，替近疾。

translations.literal: Literal: Arrogance is near disorder; decline is near illness.
translations.idiomatic: Arrogance is near disorder; decline is near illness.

sentences.zh: 君為主，其先亡虖！」

translations.literal: Literal: You are the host ruler; will you not perish first?"
translations.idiomatic: You are the host ruler; will you not perish first?"

sentences.zh: 庶徵之恆雨，劉歆以為春秋大雨也，劉向以為大水。

translations.literal: Literal: For the regular sign of persistent rain, Liu Xin took it as great rains in the Spring and Autumn, while Liu Xiang took it as great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: For the regular sign of persistent rain, Liu Xin took it as great rains in the Spring and Autumn, while Liu Xiang took it as great flooding.

sentences.zh: 隱公九年「三月癸酉，大雨，震電；

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Yin year nine: "In the third month, guiyou day, great rain with thunder and lightning;
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Yin year nine: "In the third month, guiyou day, great rain with thunder and lightning;

sentences.zh: 庚辰，大雨雪」。

translations.literal: Literal: on gengchen day, great rain and snow."
translations.idiomatic: on gengchen day, great rain and snow."

sentences.zh: 大雨，雨水也；

translations.literal: Literal: Great rain means rainwater excess;
translations.idiomatic: Great rain means rainwater excess;

sentences.zh: 震，雷也。

translations.literal: Literal: thunder means lightning-thunder.
translations.idiomatic: thunder means lightning-thunder.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為三月癸酉，於曆數春分後一日，始震電之時也，當雨，而不當大雨。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin held that guiyou day in the third month was, by calendrical count, one day after the spring equinox, the time when thunder and lightning should first arise: rain should occur, but not great rain.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that guiyou day in the third month was, by calendrical count, one day after the spring equinox, the time when thunder and lightning should first arise: rain should occur, but not great rain.

sentences.zh: 大雨，常雨之罰也。

translations.literal: Literal: Great rain is the punishment of constant rain.
translations.idiomatic: Great rain is the punishment of constant rain.

sentences.zh: 於始震電八日之間而大雨雪，常寒之罰也。

translations.literal: Literal: Within eight days from the first thunder and lightning, there came great rain and snow: this is the punishment of constant cold.
translations.idiomatic: Within eight days from the first thunder and lightning, there came great rain and snow: this is the punishment of constant cold.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為周三月，今正月也，當雨水，雪雜雨，雷電未可以發也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that in Zhou reckoning the third month is now first month: there should be rainwater; snow mixed with rain, and thunder and lightning should not yet issue.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that in Zhou reckoning the third month is now first month: there should be rainwater; snow mixed with rain, and thunder and lightning should not yet issue.

sentences.zh: 既已發也，則雪不當復降。

translations.literal: Literal: Once they have already issued, snow should not fall again.
translations.idiomatic: Once they have already issued, snow should not fall again.

sentences.zh: 皆失節，故謂之異。

translations.literal: Literal: All were out of season, therefore called anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: All were out of season, therefore called anomalies.

sentences.zh: 於易，雷以二月出，其卦日豫，言萬物隨雷出地，皆逸豫也。

translations.literal: Literal: In the Changes, thunder emerges in the second month; its hexagram day is YU, meaning all things follow thunder out of the earth and are at ease and flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, thunder emerges in the second month; its hexagram day is YU, meaning all things follow thunder out of the earth and are at ease and flourishing.

sentences.zh: 以八月入，其卦曰歸妹，言雷復歸。

translations.literal: Literal: It enters in the eighth month; its hexagram is Returning Maiden, meaning thunder returns.
translations.idiomatic: It enters in the eighth month; its hexagram is Returning Maiden, meaning thunder returns.

sentences.zh: 入地則孕毓根核，保藏蟄蟲，避盛陰之害；

translations.literal: Literal: Entering the earth, it gestates roots and kernels, preserves hibernating insects, and avoids the harm of peak yin;
translations.idiomatic: Entering the earth, it gestates roots and kernels, preserves hibernating insects, and avoids the harm of peak yin;

sentences.zh: 出地則養長華實，發揚隱伏，宣盛陽之德。

translations.literal: Literal: emerging from earth, it nourishes growth of blossom and fruit, brings forth what was hidden, and proclaims the virtue of flourishing yang.
translations.idiomatic: emerging from earth, it nourishes growth of blossom and fruit, brings forth what was hidden, and proclaims the virtue of flourishing yang.

sentences.zh: 入能除害，出能興利，人君之象也。

translations.literal: Literal: Entering can remove harm; emerging can bring benefit. This is the ruler's image.
translations.idiomatic: Entering can remove harm; emerging can bring benefit. This is the ruler's image.

sentences.zh: 是時，隱以弟桓幼，代而攝立。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Duke Yin, because his younger brother Huan was young, ruled in his place as regent.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Yin, because his younger brother Huan was young, ruled in his place as regent.

sentences.zh: 公子翬見隱居位已久，勸之遂立。

translations.literal: Literal: Prince Hui saw Yin had long occupied the position and urged him to establish himself outright.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Hui saw Yin had long occupied the position and urged him to establish himself outright.

sentences.zh: 隱既不許，翬懼而易其辭，遂與桓共殺隱。

translations.literal: Literal: Yin did not consent; Hui grew afraid and changed his words, then joined with Huan to kill Yin.
translations.idiomatic: Yin did not consent; Hui grew afraid and changed his words, then joined with Huan to kill Yin.

sentences.zh: 天見其將然，故正月大雨水而雷電。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven foresaw this would happen, therefore in first month there was great rain and flooding with thunder and lightning.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven foresaw this would happen, therefore in first month there was great rain and flooding with thunder and lightning.

sentences.zh: 是陽不閉陰，出涉危難而害萬物。

translations.literal: Literal: This was yang failing to close yin, going out and entering peril to harm the myriad things.
translations.idiomatic: This was yang failing to close yin, going out and entering peril to harm the myriad things.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，為君失時，賊弟佞臣將作亂矣。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: the ruler has lost the proper time; a treacherous younger brother and fawning ministers will make rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: the ruler has lost the proper time; a treacherous younger brother and fawning ministers will make rebellion.

sentences.zh: 後八日大雨雪，陰見間隙而勝陽，篡殺之禍將成也。

translations.literal: Literal: Eight days later came great rain and snow; yin saw a gap and overcame yang, and the disaster of usurpation and killing was about to be completed.
translations.idiomatic: Eight days later came great rain and snow; yin saw a gap and overcame yang, and the disaster of usurpation and killing was about to be completed.

sentences.zh: 公不寤，後二年而殺。

translations.literal: Literal: The duke did not awaken, and two years later was killed.
translations.idiomatic: The duke did not awaken, and two years later was killed.

sentences.zh: 昭帝始元元年七月，大水雨，自七月至十月。

translations.literal: Literal: In the seventh month of Shiyuan year one under Emperor Zhao, there was great flooding rain, from seventh month through tenth month.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of Shiyuan year one under Emperor Zhao, there was great flooding rain, from seventh month through tenth month.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始三年秋，大雨三十餘日；

translations.literal: Literal: In autumn of Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, there was heavy rain for more than thirty days.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, there was heavy rain for more than thirty days.

sentences.zh: 四年九月，大雨十餘日。

translations.literal: Literal: In the ninth month of year four, heavy rain lasted more than ten days.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month of year four, heavy rain lasted more than ten days.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳愍公二年，晉獻公使太子申生帥師，公衣之偏衣，佩之金玦。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition, Duke Min year two: Duke Xian of Jin sent the heir apparent Shen Sheng to command troops; the duke clothed him in a mixed-color robe and gave him a gold ring-pendant.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition, Duke Min year two: Duke Xian of Jin sent the heir apparent Shen Sheng to command troops; the duke clothed him in a mixed-color robe and gave him a gold ring-pendant.

sentences.zh: 狐突歎曰：「時，事之徵也；

translations.literal: Literal: Hu Tu sighed: "Timing is the sign of affairs;
translations.idiomatic: Hu Tu sighed: "Timing is the sign of affairs;

sentences.zh: 衣，身之章也；

translations.literal: Literal: clothing is the emblem of the body;
translations.idiomatic: clothing is the emblem of the body;

sentences.zh: 佩，衷之旗也。

translations.literal: Literal: pendants are the banner of one's inner heart.
translations.idiomatic: pendants are the banner of one's inner heart.

sentences.zh: 故敬其事，則命以始；

translations.literal: Literal: Thus, when one reveres the affair, one gives command with a proper beginning;
translations.idiomatic: Thus, when one reveres the affair, one gives command with a proper beginning;

sentences.zh: 服其身，則衣之純；

translations.literal: Literal: when one clothes the person, one gives pure garments;
translations.idiomatic: when one clothes the person, one gives pure garments;

sentences.zh: 用其衷，則佩之度。

translations.literal: Literal: when one uses the inner heart, one gives pendants by proper measure.
translations.idiomatic: when one uses the inner heart, one gives pendants by proper measure.

sentences.zh: 今命以時卒，閟其事也；

translations.literal: Literal: Now the command uses an ominous timing, concealing his undertaking;
translations.idiomatic: Now the command uses an ominous timing, concealing his undertaking;

sentences.zh: 衣以尨服，遠其躬也；

translations.literal: Literal: the robe is variegated dress, distancing his person;
translations.idiomatic: the robe is variegated dress, distancing his person;

sentences.zh: 佩以金玦，棄其衷也。

translations.literal: Literal: the pendant is a gold ring, casting away his inner heart.
translations.idiomatic: the pendant is a gold ring, casting away his inner heart.

sentences.zh: 服以遠之，時以閟之，尨涼冬殺，金寒玦離，胡可恃也！」

translations.literal: Literal: His dress distances him, timing conceals him; variegation is cold winter-killing, and gold ring means cold severance. How can this be relied on?"
translations.idiomatic: His dress distances him, timing conceals him; variegation is cold winter-killing, and gold ring means cold severance. How can this be relied on?"

sentences.zh: 梁餘子養曰：「帥師者，受命于廟，受脤於社，有常服矣。

translations.literal: Literal: Liangyu Ziyang said: "A commander receives command at the ancestral temple and receives sacrificial meat at the altar; there are established garments for this.
translations.idiomatic: Liangyu Ziyang said: "A commander receives command at the ancestral temple and receives sacrificial meat at the altar; there are established garments for this.

sentences.zh: 弗獲而尨，命可知也。

translations.literal: Literal: If he does not receive them and instead wears variegation, the mandate can be known.
translations.idiomatic: If he does not receive them and instead wears variegation, the mandate can be known.

sentences.zh: 死而不孝，不如逃之。」

translations.literal: Literal: To die and be unfilial is worse than fleeing."
translations.idiomatic: To die and be unfilial is worse than fleeing."

sentences.zh: 罕夷曰：「尨奇無常，金玦不復，君有心矣。」

translations.literal: Literal: Han Yi said: "Variegation is strange and without constancy; a gold ring means no return. The ruler has intent already."
translations.idiomatic: Han Yi said: "Variegation is strange and without constancy; a gold ring means no return. The ruler has intent already."

sentences.zh: 後四年，申生以讒自殺。

translations.literal: Literal: Four years later, Shen Sheng killed himself because of slander.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later, Shen Sheng killed himself because of slander.

sentences.zh: 近服妖也。

translations.literal: Literal: This was a near clothing portent.
translations.idiomatic: This was a near clothing portent.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰，鄭子臧好聚鷸冠，鄭文公惡之，使盜殺之。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition says that Zizang of Zheng liked gathering kingfisher-feather caps; Duke Wen of Zheng hated this and had him assassinated by thieves.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says that Zizang of Zheng liked gathering kingfisher-feather caps; Duke Wen of Zheng hated this and had him assassinated by thieves.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近服妖者也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near clothing portent.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near clothing portent.

sentences.zh: 一曰，非獨為子臧之身，亦文公之戒也。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says this was not only for Zizang himself, but also a warning to Duke Wen.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says this was not only for Zizang himself, but also a warning to Duke Wen.

sentences.zh: 初，文公不禮晉文，又犯天子命而伐滑，不尊尊敬上。

translations.literal: Literal: At first Duke Wen showed no proper rites to Duke Wen of Jin, and again violated the Son of Heaven's command by attacking Hua, failing to honor those above.
translations.idiomatic: At first Duke Wen showed no proper rites to Duke Wen of Jin, and again violated the Son of Heaven's command by attacking Hua, failing to honor those above.

sentences.zh: 其後晉文伐鄭，幾亡國。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward Duke Wen of Jin attacked Zheng, and the state nearly perished.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Duke Wen of Jin attacked Zheng, and the state nearly perished.

sentences.zh: 昭帝時，昌邑王賀遣中大夫之長安，多治仄注冠，以賜大臣，又以冠奴。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Zhao's time, King He of Changyi sent central grandees to Chang'an, made many slanted caps, gave them to high ministers, and even capped slaves.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Zhao's time, King He of Changyi sent central grandees to Chang'an, made many slanted caps, gave them to high ministers, and even capped slaves.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近服妖也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near clothing portent.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near clothing portent.

sentences.zh: 時王賀狂悖，聞天子不豫，弋獵馳騁如故，與騶奴宰人游居娛戲，驕嫚不敬。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time King He was reckless and perverse: hearing the Son of Heaven was ill, he still hunted and galloped as before, roaming and amusing himself with grooms, slaves, and cooks, arrogant and irreverent.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King He was reckless and perverse: hearing the Son of Heaven was ill, he still hunted and galloped as before, roaming and amusing himself with grooms, slaves, and cooks, arrogant and irreverent.

sentences.zh: 冠者尊服，奴者賤人，賀無故好作非常之冠，暴尊象也。

translations.literal: Literal: Caps are garments of rank and slaves are base people. He, without cause, delighted in making abnormal caps, violating symbols of rank.
translations.idiomatic: Caps are garments of rank and slaves are base people. He, without cause, delighted in making abnormal caps, violating symbols of rank.

sentences.zh: 以冠奴者，當自至尊墜至賤也。

translations.literal: Literal: To cap slaves means that one at the highest rank will fall to the lowest.
translations.idiomatic: To cap slaves means that one at the highest rank will fall to the lowest.

sentences.zh: 其後帝崩，無子，漢大臣徵賀為嗣。

translations.literal: Literal: Later the emperor died childless, and Han ministers summoned He as heir.
translations.idiomatic: Later the emperor died childless, and Han ministers summoned He as heir.

sentences.zh: 即位，狂亂無道，縛戮諫者夏侯勝等。

translations.literal: Literal: After enthronement he was mad and lawless, binding and executing remonstrants such as Xiahou Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: After enthronement he was mad and lawless, binding and executing remonstrants such as Xiahou Sheng.

sentences.zh: 於是大臣白皇太后，廢賀為庶人。

translations.literal: Literal: Thereupon high ministers informed the empress dowager, and He was deposed to commoner status.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon high ministers informed the empress dowager, and He was deposed to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 賀為王時，又見大白狗冠方山冠而無尾，此服妖，亦犬禍也。

translations.literal: Literal: When He was still king, he also saw a large white dog wearing a square-shan cap and lacking a tail. This was a clothing portent and also a dog calamity.
translations.idiomatic: When He was still king, he also saw a large white dog wearing a square-shan cap and lacking a tail. This was a clothing portent and also a dog calamity.

sentences.zh: 賀以問郎中令龔遂，遂曰：「此天戒，言在仄者盡冠狗也。

translations.literal: Literal: He asked Chamberlain Gong Sui about it. Sui said: "This is Heaven's warning, saying all in crooked positions will be crowned as dogs.
translations.idiomatic: He asked Chamberlain Gong Sui about it. Sui said: "This is Heaven's warning, saying all in crooked positions will be crowned as dogs.

sentences.zh: 去之則存，不去則亡矣。」

translations.literal: Literal: Remove it and you survive; do not remove it and you perish."
translations.idiomatic: Remove it and you survive; do not remove it and you perish."

sentences.zh: 賀既廢數年，宣帝封之為列侯，復有罪，死不得置後，又犬禍無尾之效也。

translations.literal: Literal: Several years after He was deposed, Emperor Xuan enfeoffed him as a marquis; he committed another crime and died without heir, again confirming the tailless dog calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Several years after He was deposed, Emperor Xuan enfeoffed him as a marquis; he committed another crime and died without heir, again confirming the tailless dog calamity.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「行不順，厥咎人奴冠，天下亂，辟無適，妾子拜。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If conduct is not compliant, the fault-sign is slaves wearing caps; under heaven there is disorder, punishments misapplied, and concubines' sons receive obeisance."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If conduct is not compliant, the fault-sign is slaves wearing caps; under heaven there is disorder, punishments misapplied, and concubines' sons receive obeisance."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「君不正，臣欲篡，厥妖狗冠出朝門。」

translations.literal: Literal: It also says: "When the ruler is not upright and ministers wish to usurp, the portent is a cap-wearing dog appearing at the court gate."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "When the ruler is not upright and ministers wish to usurp, the portent is a cap-wearing dog appearing at the court gate."

sentences.zh: 成帝鴻嘉、永始之間，好為微行出游，選從期門郎有材力者，及私奴客，多至十餘，少五六人，皆白衣袒幘，帶持刀劍。

translations.literal: Literal: Between Hongjia and Yongshi under Emperor Cheng, he liked to travel in disguise, choosing attendants from strong and capable palace guards, plus private slaves and retainers, often over ten, sometimes five or six, all in white clothes with bare headcloths and swords at their belts.
translations.idiomatic: Between Hongjia and Yongshi under Emperor Cheng, he liked to travel in disguise, choosing attendants from strong and capable palace guards, plus private slaves and retainers, often over ten, sometimes five or six, all in white clothes with bare headcloths and swords at their belts.

sentences.zh: 或乘小車，御者在茵上，或皆騎，出入市里郊野，遠至旁縣。

translations.literal: Literal: Some rode small carriages with the driver seated on a mat; others all rode horses, going in and out of markets, lanes, and suburbs, even to neighboring counties.
translations.idiomatic: Some rode small carriages with the driver seated on a mat; others all rode horses, going in and out of markets, lanes, and suburbs, even to neighboring counties.

sentences.zh: 時，大臣車騎將軍王音及劉向等數以切諫。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time high ministers, including General of Chariots and Cavalry Wang Yin and Liu Xiang, repeatedly offered urgent remonstrance.
translations.idiomatic: At that time high ministers, including General of Chariots and Cavalry Wang Yin and Liu Xiang, repeatedly offered urgent remonstrance.

sentences.zh: 谷永曰：「易稱『得臣無家』，言王者臣天下，無私家也。

translations.literal: Literal: Gu Yong said: "The Changes says, "Gaining ministers, no private household," meaning the king takes all under heaven as his charge and has no private house.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong said: "The Changes says, "Gaining ministers, no private household," meaning the king takes all under heaven as his charge and has no private house.

sentences.zh: 今陛下棄萬乘之至貴，樂家人之賤事；

translations.literal: Literal: Now Your Majesty abandons the utmost nobility of ten thousand chariots and delights in the base activities of householders;
translations.idiomatic: Now Your Majesty abandons the utmost nobility of ten thousand chariots and delights in the base activities of householders;

sentences.zh: 厭高美之尊稱，好匹夫之卑字；

translations.literal: Literal: you tire of the lofty and beautiful imperial titles and like the low names of common men;
translations.idiomatic: you tire of the lofty and beautiful imperial titles and like the low names of common men;

sentences.zh: 崇聚票輕無誼之人，以為私客；

translations.literal: Literal: you gather rootless and frivolous men without principle and make them private retainers;
translations.idiomatic: you gather rootless and frivolous men without principle and make them private retainers;

sentences.zh: 置私田於民間，畜私奴車馬於北宮；

translations.literal: Literal: you set private fields among the people and keep private slaves, carriages, and horses in the North Palace;
translations.idiomatic: you set private fields among the people and keep private slaves, carriages, and horses in the North Palace;

sentences.zh: 數去南面之尊，離深宮之固，挺身獨與小人晨夜相隨，烏集醉飽吏民之家，亂服共坐，溷肴亡別，閔勉遯樂，晝夜在路。

translations.literal: Literal: repeatedly leaving the southern-facing sovereign seat and the security of the deep palace, you go alone with petty men day and night, flock like crows to drunken feasts in officials' and commoners' houses, wearing mixed dress and sitting together in disorder, muddling food and rank without distinction, pursuing furtive pleasures and evasion, and staying on the roads day and night.
translations.idiomatic: repeatedly leaving the southern-facing sovereign seat and the security of the deep palace, you go alone with petty men day and night, flock like crows to drunken feasts in officials' and commoners' houses, wearing mixed dress and sitting together in disorder, muddling food and rank without distinction, pursuing furtive pleasures and evasion, and staying on the roads day and night.

sentences.zh: 典門戶奉宿衛之臣執干戈守空宮，公卿百寮不知陛下所在，積數年矣。

translations.literal: Literal: The ministers who guard gates and night watch hold halberds to defend an empty palace, while dukes, ministers, and all officials do not know where Your Majesty is, and this has gone on for years.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers who guard gates and night watch hold halberds to defend an empty palace, while dukes, ministers, and all officials do not know where Your Majesty is, and this has gone on for years.

sentences.zh: 昔虢公為無道，有神降曰『賜爾土田』，言將以庶人受土田也。

translations.literal: Literal: Formerly the Duke of Guo ruled without the Way; a spirit descended and said, "I grant you fields," meaning he would receive land as a commoner.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly the Duke of Guo ruled without the Way; a spirit descended and said, "I grant you fields," meaning he would receive land as a commoner.

sentences.zh: 諸侯夢得土田，為失國祥，而況王者畜私田財物，為庶人之事乎！」

translations.literal: Literal: When feudal lords dream of receiving fields, it is an omen of losing their state. How much more for a king to keep private fields and goods, the business of commoners!"
translations.idiomatic: When feudal lords dream of receiving fields, it is an omen of losing their state. How much more for a king to keep private fields and goods, the business of commoners!"

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰，周景王時大夫賓起見雄雞自斷其尾。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition says that in King Jing of Zhou's time, the grandee Bin Qi saw a rooster bite off its own tail.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says that in King Jing of Zhou's time, the grandee Bin Qi saw a rooster bite off its own tail.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近雞禍也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang considered this a near fowl calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang considered this a near fowl calamity.

sentences.zh: 是時，王有愛子子晁，王與賓起陰謀欲立之。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time the king had a favored son, Zichao, and the king and Bin Qi secretly plotted to install him.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the king had a favored son, Zichao, and the king and Bin Qi secretly plotted to install him.

sentences.zh: 田于北山，將因兵眾殺適子之黨，未及而崩。

translations.literal: Literal: While hunting at North Mountain, they intended to use assembled troops to kill the faction of the legitimate heir, but the king died before it happened.
translations.idiomatic: While hunting at North Mountain, they intended to use assembled troops to kill the faction of the legitimate heir, but the king died before it happened.

sentences.zh: 三子爭國，王室大亂。

translations.literal: Literal: The three sons contended for the state, and the royal house fell into great disorder.
translations.idiomatic: The three sons contended for the state, and the royal house fell into great disorder.

sentences.zh: 其後，賓起誅死，子晁奔楚而敗。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward Bin Qi was executed, and Zichao fled to Chu and was defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Bin Qi was executed, and Zichao fled to Chu and was defeated.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「有始無終，厥妖雄雞自齧斷其尾。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Having a beginning without an end: its portent is a rooster biting off its own tail."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Having a beginning without an end: its portent is a rooster biting off its own tail."

sentences.zh: 成公七年「正月，鼷鼠食郊牛角；

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Cheng year seven: "In first month, field mice gnawed the horns of the suburban sacrificial ox;
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng year seven: "In first month, field mice gnawed the horns of the suburban sacrificial ox;

sentences.zh: 改卜牛，又食其角」。

translations.literal: Literal: after divining and replacing the ox, they again gnawed its horns."
translations.idiomatic: after divining and replacing the ox, they again gnawed its horns."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近青祥，亦牛禍也，不敬而傋霿之所致也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near green portent and also an ox calamity, caused by irreverence and confused darkness in preparedness.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near green portent and also an ox calamity, caused by irreverence and confused darkness in preparedness.

sentences.zh: 昔周公制禮樂，成周道，故成王命魯郊祀天地，以尊周公。

translations.literal: Literal: Formerly the Duke of Zhou established rites and music and completed the Zhou Way, so King Cheng ordered Lu to perform suburban sacrifices to Heaven and Earth in order to honor the Duke of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly the Duke of Zhou established rites and music and completed the Zhou Way, so King Cheng ordered Lu to perform suburban sacrifices to Heaven and Earth in order to honor the Duke of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 至成公時，三家始顓政，魯將從此衰。

translations.literal: Literal: By Duke Cheng's time, the Three Families first monopolized power, and Lu would decline from this point.
translations.idiomatic: By Duke Cheng's time, the Three Families first monopolized power, and Lu would decline from this point.

sentences.zh: 天愍周公之德，痛其將有敗亡之禍，故於郊祭而見戒云。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven pitied the Duke of Zhou's virtue and grieved the coming disaster of ruin, therefore showed warning at the suburban sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven pitied the Duke of Zhou's virtue and grieved the coming disaster of ruin, therefore showed warning at the suburban sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 鼠，小蟲，性盜竊，鼷又其小者也。

translations.literal: Literal: The mouse is a tiny creature with a thieving nature; the field mouse is smaller still.
translations.idiomatic: The mouse is a tiny creature with a thieving nature; the field mouse is smaller still.

sentences.zh: 牛，大畜，祭天尊物也。

translations.literal: Literal: The ox is a great domestic animal, an honored object for sacrifice to Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The ox is a great domestic animal, an honored object for sacrifice to Heaven.

sentences.zh: 角，兵象，在上，君威也。

translations.literal: Literal: Horns symbolize arms; being above, they symbolize rulerly authority.
translations.idiomatic: Horns symbolize arms; being above, they symbolize rulerly authority.

sentences.zh: 小小鼷鼠，食至尊之牛角，象季氏乃陪臣盜竊之人，將執國命以傷君威而害周公之祀也。

translations.literal: Literal: Tiny field mice gnawing the supreme sacrificial ox's horns symbolized the Ji clan, mere attendant ministers and thieves, about to seize state command, injure rulerly authority, and damage the Duke of Zhou's sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: Tiny field mice gnawing the supreme sacrificial ox's horns symbolized the Ji clan, mere attendant ministers and thieves, about to seize state command, injure rulerly authority, and damage the Duke of Zhou's sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 改卜牛，鼷鼠又食其角，天重語之也。

translations.literal: Literal: After divining and changing the ox, field mice again gnawed its horns: Heaven repeated its warning.
translations.idiomatic: After divining and changing the ox, field mice again gnawed its horns: Heaven repeated its warning.

sentences.zh: 成公怠慢昏亂，遂君臣更執于晉。

translations.literal: Literal: Duke Cheng was negligent, arrogant, and confused; ruler and ministers were then successively held by Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Cheng was negligent, arrogant, and confused; ruler and ministers were then successively held by Jin.

sentences.zh: 至于襄公，晉為溴梁之會，天下大夫皆奪君政。

translations.literal: Literal: By Duke Xiang's time, Jin held the Hui at Chouliang, and great officers across the realm all seized their rulers' power.
translations.idiomatic: By Duke Xiang's time, Jin held the Hui at Chouliang, and great officers across the realm all seized their rulers' power.

sentences.zh: 其後三家逐昭公，卒死于外，幾絕周公之祀。

translations.literal: Literal: Later the Three Families expelled Duke Zhao, who eventually died abroad, nearly cutting off the Duke of Zhou's sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: Later the Three Families expelled Duke Zhao, who eventually died abroad, nearly cutting off the Duke of Zhou's sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為鼷鼠食郊牛，皆養牲不謹也。

translations.literal: Literal: Dong Zhongshu took mice gnawing the suburban ox as entirely due to careless raising of sacrificial animals.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu took mice gnawing the suburban ox as entirely due to careless raising of sacrificial animals.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「祭天不慎，厥妖鼷鼠齧郊牛角。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If sacrifice to Heaven is not careful, the portent is field mice gnawing the suburban ox's horns."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If sacrifice to Heaven is not careful, the portent is field mice gnawing the suburban ox's horns."

sentences.zh: 哀公元年「正月，鼷鼠食郊牛」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Ai year one: "In first month, field mice gnawed the suburban ox."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai year one: "In first month, field mice gnawed the suburban ox."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為天意汲汲於用聖人，逐三家，故復見戒也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that Heaven urgently intended the use of a sage to expel the Three Families, therefore the warning appeared again.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that Heaven urgently intended the use of a sage to expel the Three Families, therefore the warning appeared again.

sentences.zh: 哀公年少，不親見昭公之事，故見敗亡之異。

translations.literal: Literal: Duke Ai was young and had not personally seen Duke Zhao's affairs, so this omen of defeat and ruin appeared.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ai was young and had not personally seen Duke Zhao's affairs, so this omen of defeat and ruin appeared.

sentences.zh: 已而哀不寤，身奔於粵，此其效也。

translations.literal: Literal: Soon Duke Ai did not awaken, fled to Yue, and this became its fulfillment.
translations.idiomatic: Soon Duke Ai did not awaken, fled to Yue, and this became its fulfillment.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始四年九月，長安城南有鼠銜黃蒿、柏葉，上民冢柏及榆樹上為巢，桐柏尤多。

translations.literal: Literal: In ninth month of Jianshi year four under Emperor Cheng, south of Chang'an city there were mice carrying yellow mugwort and cypress leaves, nesting atop cypresses on people's graves and on elm trees, especially on paulownia and cypress.
translations.idiomatic: In ninth month of Jianshi year four under Emperor Cheng, south of Chang'an city there were mice carrying yellow mugwort and cypress leaves, nesting atop cypresses on people's graves and on elm trees, especially on paulownia and cypress.

sentences.zh: 巢中無子，皆有乾鼠矢數十。

translations.literal: Literal: There were no young in the nests; each contained dozens of dry mouse droppings.
translations.idiomatic: There were no young in the nests; each contained dozens of dry mouse droppings.

sentences.zh: 時議臣以為恐有水災。

translations.literal: Literal: Court discussants then said they feared a flood disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Court discussants then said they feared a flood disaster.

sentences.zh: 鼠，盜竊小蟲，夜出晝匿；

translations.literal: Literal: Mice are thieving little creatures, active at night and hidden by day;
translations.idiomatic: Mice are thieving little creatures, active at night and hidden by day;

sentences.zh: 今晝去穴而登木，象賤人將居顯貴之位也。

translations.literal: Literal: now they left burrows by day and climbed trees, symbolizing low people about to occupy conspicuous high rank.
