translations.idiomatic: now they left burrows by day and climbed trees, symbolizing low people about to occupy conspicuous high rank.

sentences.zh: 桐柏，衛思后園所在也。

translations.literal: Literal: Paulownia and cypress marked the location of Empress Wei Si's garden.
translations.idiomatic: Paulownia and cypress marked the location of Empress Wei Si's garden.

sentences.zh: 其後，趙皇后自微賤登至尊，與衛后同類。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward Empress Zhao rose from humble status to highest rank, of the same type as Empress Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Empress Zhao rose from humble status to highest rank, of the same type as Empress Wei.

sentences.zh: 趙后終無子而為害。

translations.literal: Literal: Empress Zhao ended childless and caused harm.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Zhao ended childless and caused harm.

sentences.zh: 明年，有鳶焚巢，殺子之異也。

translations.literal: Literal: The following year there was the anomaly of kites burning nests, meaning killing offspring.
translations.idiomatic: The following year there was the anomaly of kites burning nests, meaning killing offspring.

sentences.zh: 天象仍見，甚可畏也。

translations.literal: Literal: Heavenly signs appeared repeatedly; it was greatly to be feared.
translations.idiomatic: Heavenly signs appeared repeatedly; it was greatly to be feared.

sentences.zh: 一曰，皆王莽竊位之象云。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says all these signaled Wang Mang's usurpation.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says all these signaled Wang Mang's usurpation.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「臣私祿罔辟，厥妖鼠巢。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers hold private stipends and ignore rightful office, the portent is mouse nests."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers hold private stipends and ignore rightful office, the portent is mouse nests."

sentences.zh: 景帝三年十二月，吳二城門自傾，大船自覆。

translations.literal: Literal: In twelfth month of Emperor Jing year three, two city gates of Wu collapsed by themselves, and large ships overturned by themselves.
translations.idiomatic: In twelfth month of Emperor Jing year three, two city gates of Wu collapsed by themselves, and large ships overturned by themselves.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近金沴木，木動也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took this as metal qi harming wood, namely wood in agitation.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as metal qi harming wood, namely wood in agitation.

sentences.zh: 先是，吳王濞以太子死於漢，稱疾不朝，陰與楚王戊謀為逆亂。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, King Pi of Wu, because his heir had died in Han, claimed illness and did not come to court, secretly plotting rebellion with King Wu of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, King Pi of Wu, because his heir had died in Han, claimed illness and did not come to court, secretly plotting rebellion with King Wu of Chu.

sentences.zh: 城猶國也，其一門名曰楚門，一門曰魚門。

translations.literal: Literal: A city is like a state; one gate was called Chu Gate, one Fish Gate.
translations.idiomatic: A city is like a state; one gate was called Chu Gate, one Fish Gate.

sentences.zh: 吳地以船為家，以魚為食。

translations.literal: Literal: In Wu, people took ships as homes and fish as food.
translations.idiomatic: In Wu, people took ships as homes and fish as food.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，與楚所謀，傾國覆家。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: in plotting with Chu, you will topple state and overturn house.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: in plotting with Chu, you will topple state and overturn house.

sentences.zh: 吳王不寤，正月，與楚俱起兵，身死國亡。

translations.literal: Literal: The king of Wu did not awaken; in first month he raised troops with Chu, and he died while his state perished.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Wu did not awaken; in first month he raised troops with Chu, and he died while his state perished.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「上下咸誖，厥妖城門壞。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When above and below are all perverse, the portent is ruined city gates."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When above and below are all perverse, the portent is ruined city gates."

sentences.zh: 宣帝時，大司馬霍禹所居第門自壞。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Xuan's time, the gate of the residence of Grand Marshal Huo Yu collapsed by itself.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Xuan's time, the gate of the residence of Grand Marshal Huo Yu collapsed by itself.

sentences.zh: 時禹內不順，外不敬，見戒不改，卒受滅亡之誅。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Yu was inwardly disobedient and outwardly irreverent; seeing warning, he did not change, and in the end suffered extermination.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Yu was inwardly disobedient and outwardly irreverent; seeing warning, he did not change, and in the end suffered extermination.

sentences.zh: 哀帝時，大司馬董賢第門自壞。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Ai's time, the gate of the residence of Grand Marshal Dong Xian collapsed by itself.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Ai's time, the gate of the residence of Grand Marshal Dong Xian collapsed by itself.

sentences.zh: 時賢以私愛居大位，賞賜無度，驕嫚不敬，大失臣道，見戒不改。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Xian held high office through private favor, with boundless rewards, arrogant and irreverent, greatly failing a minister's way; seeing warning, he did not change.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Xian held high office through private favor, with boundless rewards, arrogant and irreverent, greatly failing a minister's way; seeing warning, he did not change.

sentences.zh: 後賢夫妻自殺，家徙合浦。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward Xian and his wife committed suicide, and their household was relocated to Hepu.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Xian and his wife committed suicide, and their household was relocated to Hepu.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「言之不從，是謂不艾，厥咎僭，厥罰恆陽，厥極憂。

translations.literal: Literal: The Tradition says: "When speech is not heeded, this is called lack of order; its fault is transgression, its punishment is constant sunshine, and its extreme is anxiety.
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "When speech is not heeded, this is called lack of order; its fault is transgression, its punishment is constant sunshine, and its extreme is anxiety.

sentences.zh: 時則有詩妖，時則有介蟲之孽，時則有犬禍，時則有口舌之痾，時則有白眚白祥。

translations.literal: Literal: Then at times there are poetic anomalies, at times prodigies of armored insects, at times dog calamities, at times afflictions of mouth and tongue, and at times white blights and white portents.
translations.idiomatic: Then at times there are poetic anomalies, at times prodigies of armored insects, at times dog calamities, at times afflictions of mouth and tongue, and at times white blights and white portents.

sentences.zh: 惟木沴金。」

translations.literal: Literal: Only Wood injures Metal."
translations.idiomatic: Only Wood injures Metal."

sentences.zh: 「言之不從」，從，順也。

translations.literal: Literal: "Speech not heeded": cong means compliance.
translations.idiomatic: "Speech not heeded": cong means compliance.

sentences.zh: 「是謂不乂」，乂，治也。

translations.literal: Literal: "This is called not ordered": yi means governance.
translations.idiomatic: "This is called not ordered": yi means governance.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「君子居其室，出其言不善，則千里之外違之，況其邇者虖！」

translations.literal: Literal: Confucius said: "If a gentleman, in his chamber, sends out words that are not good, those a thousand li away will oppose them, how much more those near at hand!"
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: "If a gentleman, in his chamber, sends out words that are not good, those a thousand li away will oppose them, how much more those near at hand!"

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「如蜩如螗，如沸如羹。」

translations.literal: Literal: The Odes says: "Like cicadas, like dragonflies, like boiling, like soup."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "Like cicadas, like dragonflies, like boiling, like soup."

sentences.zh: 言上號令不順民心，虛譁憒亂，則不能治海內，失在過差，故其咎僭。

translations.literal: Literal: When commands from above do not accord with people's hearts, and there is empty clamor and confusion, one cannot govern the realm; the fault lies in excess and error, so its blame is transgression.
translations.idiomatic: When commands from above do not accord with people's hearts, and there is empty clamor and confusion, one cannot govern the realm; the fault lies in excess and error, so its blame is transgression.

sentences.zh: 僭，差也。

translations.literal: Literal: Transgression means deviation.
translations.idiomatic: Transgression means deviation.

sentences.zh: 刑罰妄加，群陰不附，則陽氣勝，故其罰常陽也。

translations.literal: Literal: When punishments are arbitrarily imposed and the many yin do not attach, yang qi prevails; therefore the punishment is constant sunshine.
translations.idiomatic: When punishments are arbitrarily imposed and the many yin do not attach, yang qi prevails; therefore the punishment is constant sunshine.

sentences.zh: 旱傷百穀，則有寇難，上下俱憂，故其極憂也。

translations.literal: Literal: Drought harms the hundred grains, then there are bandit troubles and both above and below are anxious; therefore its extreme is anxiety.
translations.idiomatic: Drought harms the hundred grains, then there are bandit troubles and both above and below are anxious; therefore its extreme is anxiety.

sentences.zh: 君炕陽而暴虐，臣畏刑而柑口，則怨謗之氣發於歌謠，故有詩妖。

translations.literal: Literal: If the ruler exalts harsh yang and is tyrannical, and ministers fear punishment and clamp their mouths shut, then resentful slanderous qi emerges in songs and ballads; therefore there are poetic anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: If the ruler exalts harsh yang and is tyrannical, and ministers fear punishment and clamp their mouths shut, then resentful slanderous qi emerges in songs and ballads; therefore there are poetic anomalies.

sentences.zh: 介蟲孽者，謂小蟲有甲飛揚之類，陽氣所生也，於春秋為螽，今謂之蝗，皆其類也。

translations.literal: Literal: Prodigies of armored insects means small insects with shells that fly and swarm, generated by yang qi; in the Spring and Autumn they are called chong, now called locusts, all of this kind.
translations.idiomatic: Prodigies of armored insects means small insects with shells that fly and swarm, generated by yang qi; in the Spring and Autumn they are called chong, now called locusts, all of this kind.

sentences.zh: 於易，兌為口，犬以吠守，而不可信，言氣毀故有犬禍。

translations.literal: Literal: In the Changes, Dui is mouth; dogs guard by barking yet are not trustworthy. Since the qi of speech is damaged, therefore there are dog calamities.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, Dui is mouth; dogs guard by barking yet are not trustworthy. Since the qi of speech is damaged, therefore there are dog calamities.

sentences.zh: 一曰，旱歲犬多狂死及為怪，亦是也。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says: in drought years, many dogs go mad and die or become strange; this also belongs here.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: in drought years, many dogs go mad and die or become strange; this also belongs here.

sentences.zh: 及人，則多病口喉欬者，故有口舌痾。

translations.literal: Literal: When it reaches people, there are many illnesses of mouth, throat, and cough; therefore there are afflictions of mouth and tongue.
translations.idiomatic: When it reaches people, there are many illnesses of mouth, throat, and cough; therefore there are afflictions of mouth and tongue.

sentences.zh: 金色白，故有白眚白祥。

translations.literal: Literal: Metal's color is white; therefore there are white blights and white portents.
translations.idiomatic: Metal's color is white; therefore there are white blights and white portents.

sentences.zh: 凡言傷者，病金氣；

translations.literal: Literal: In all cases where speech is harmed, metal qi is ill;
translations.idiomatic: In all cases where speech is harmed, metal qi is ill;

sentences.zh: 金氣病，則木沴之。

translations.literal: Literal: when metal qi is ill, wood injures it.
translations.idiomatic: when metal qi is ill, wood injures it.

sentences.zh: 其極憂者，順之，其福曰康寧，劉歆言傳曰時有毛蟲之孽。

translations.literal: Literal: As for its extreme anxiety, if one follows it, the blessing is called health and peace. Liu Xin's tradition says there are prodigies of hairy insects.
translations.idiomatic: As for its extreme anxiety, if one follows it, the blessing is called health and peace. Liu Xin's tradition says there are prodigies of hairy insects.

sentences.zh: 說以為於天文西方參為虎星，故為毛蟲。

translations.literal: Literal: The explanation says that in astronomy, Shen in the west is the Tiger star, therefore hairy insects.
translations.idiomatic: The explanation says that in astronomy, Shen in the west is the Tiger star, therefore hairy insects.

sentences.zh: 史記周單襄公與晉郤錡、郤犨、郤至、齊國佐語，告魯成公曰：「晉將有亂，三郤其當之虖！

translations.literal: Literal: In the Records, Duke Shan Xiang of Zhou spoke with Jin's Xi Qi, Xi Chou, Xi Zhi, and Guo Zuo of Qi, and told Duke Cheng of Lu: "Jin will have disorder; the three Xi will bear it!
translations.idiomatic: In the Records, Duke Shan Xiang of Zhou spoke with Jin's Xi Qi, Xi Chou, Xi Zhi, and Guo Zuo of Qi, and told Duke Cheng of Lu: "Jin will have disorder; the three Xi will bear it!

sentences.zh: 夫郤氏，晉之寵人也，二卿而五大夫，可以戒懼矣。

translations.literal: Literal: The Xi clan are Jin's favored men: two high ministers and five great officers. They should have been cautious.
translations.idiomatic: The Xi clan are Jin's favored men: two high ministers and five great officers. They should have been cautious.

sentences.zh: 高位實疾顛，厚味實腊毒。

translations.literal: Literal: High position truly hastens a fall; rich flavor truly becomes poison.
translations.idiomatic: High position truly hastens a fall; rich flavor truly becomes poison.

sentences.zh: 今郤伯之語犯，叔迂，季伐。

translations.literal: Literal: Now the senior Xi's words are offensive, the middle one is crooked, the youngest is aggressive.
translations.idiomatic: Now the senior Xi's words are offensive, the middle one is crooked, the youngest is aggressive.

sentences.zh: 犯則陵人，迂則誣人，伐則掩人。

translations.literal: Literal: Offensive means bullying others; crooked means slandering others; aggressive means suppressing others.
translations.idiomatic: Offensive means bullying others; crooked means slandering others; aggressive means suppressing others.

sentences.zh: 有是寵也，而益之以三怨，其誰能忍之！

translations.literal: Literal: With such favor, and adding three hatreds to it, who can endure them?
translations.idiomatic: With such favor, and adding three hatreds to it, who can endure them?

sentences.zh: 雖齊國子亦將與焉。

translations.literal: Literal: Even Guozi of Qi will also be involved.
translations.idiomatic: Even Guozi of Qi will also be involved.

sentences.zh: 立於淫亂之國，而好盡言以招人過，怨之本也。

translations.literal: Literal: Standing in a licentious and disordered state, yet loving to speak fully and expose others' faults, is the root of resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Standing in a licentious and disordered state, yet loving to speak fully and expose others' faults, is the root of resentment.

sentences.zh: 唯善人能受盡言，齊其有虖？」

translations.literal: Literal: Only good men can receive blunt speech. Does Qi have such men?"
translations.idiomatic: Only good men can receive blunt speech. Does Qi have such men?"

sentences.zh: 十七年，晉殺三郤。

translations.literal: Literal: In year seventeen, Jin killed the three Xi.
translations.idiomatic: In year seventeen, Jin killed the three Xi.

sentences.zh: 十八年，齊殺國佐。

translations.literal: Literal: In year eighteen, Qi killed Guo Zuo.
translations.idiomatic: In year eighteen, Qi killed Guo Zuo.

sentences.zh: 凡此屬，皆言不從之咎云。

translations.literal: Literal: All cases like these are said to be blame from speech not being heeded.
translations.idiomatic: All cases like these are said to be blame from speech not being heeded.

sentences.zh: 晉穆侯以條之役生太子，名之曰仇；

translations.literal: Literal: Marquis Mu of Jin, after the campaign at Tiao, had a son and named the heir Chou.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Mu of Jin, after the campaign at Tiao, had a son and named the heir Chou.

sentences.zh: 其弟以千畝之戰生，名之曰成師。

translations.literal: Literal: His younger son, born after the battle of Qianmu, was named Chengshi.
translations.idiomatic: His younger son, born after the battle of Qianmu, was named Chengshi.

sentences.zh: 師服曰：「異哉，君之名子也！

translations.literal: Literal: Shi Fu said: "Strange indeed, my lord's naming of sons!
translations.idiomatic: Shi Fu said: "Strange indeed, my lord's naming of sons!

sentences.zh: 夫名以制誼，誼以出禮，禮以體政，政以正民，是以政成而民聽；

translations.literal: Literal: Names regulate rightness, rightness gives rise to ritual, ritual embodies governance, governance rectifies the people; thus governance succeeds and the people obey.
translations.idiomatic: Names regulate rightness, rightness gives rise to ritual, ritual embodies governance, governance rectifies the people; thus governance succeeds and the people obey.

sentences.zh: 易則生亂。

translations.literal: Literal: If changed, disorder is born.
translations.idiomatic: If changed, disorder is born.

sentences.zh: 嘉耦曰妃，怨耦曰仇，古之命也。

translations.literal: Literal: A good pair is called spouse; a resentful pair is called enemy. This is ancient naming.
translations.idiomatic: A good pair is called spouse; a resentful pair is called enemy. This is ancient naming.

sentences.zh: 今君名太子曰仇，弟曰成師，始兆亂矣，兄其替虖！」

translations.literal: Literal: Now my lord names the heir Enemy and the younger son Completed Army: this already signals disorder. Will the elder be deposed?"
translations.idiomatic: Now my lord names the heir Enemy and the younger son Completed Army: this already signals disorder. Will the elder be deposed?"

sentences.zh: 及仇嗣立，是為文侯。

translations.literal: Literal: When Chou succeeded, he was Marquis Wen.
translations.idiomatic: When Chou succeeded, he was Marquis Wen.

sentences.zh: 文侯卒，子昭侯立，封成師于曲沃，號桓叔。

translations.literal: Literal: Marquis Wen died; his son Marquis Zhao was installed and enfeoffed Chengshi at Quwo, styled Huan Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Wen died; his son Marquis Zhao was installed and enfeoffed Chengshi at Quwo, styled Huan Shu.

sentences.zh: 後晉人殺昭侯而納桓叔，不克。

translations.literal: Literal: Later Jin men killed Marquis Zhao and tried to install Huan Shu, but failed.
translations.idiomatic: Later Jin men killed Marquis Zhao and tried to install Huan Shu, but failed.

sentences.zh: 復立昭侯子孝侯，桓叔子嚴伯殺之。

translations.literal: Literal: They restored Marquis Zhao's son Marquis Xiao; then Huan Shu's son Yanbo killed him.
translations.idiomatic: They restored Marquis Zhao's son Marquis Xiao; then Huan Shu's son Yanbo killed him.

sentences.zh: 晉人立其弟鄂侯。

translations.literal: Literal: The Jin people installed his younger brother, Marquis E.
translations.idiomatic: The Jin people installed his younger brother, Marquis E.

sentences.zh: 鄂侯生哀侯，嚴伯子武公復殺哀侯及其弟，滅之，而代有晉國。

translations.literal: Literal: Marquis E fathered Marquis Ai; then Yanbo's son Duke Wu again killed Marquis Ai and his younger brother, extinguished their line, and took Jin's state for himself.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis E fathered Marquis Ai; then Yanbo's son Duke Wu again killed Marquis Ai and his younger brother, extinguished their line, and took Jin's state for himself.

sentences.zh: 宣公六年，鄭公子曼滿與王子伯廖語，欲為卿。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xuan year six, Prince Manman of Zheng spoke with Prince Boliao, wanting to become a high minister.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year six, Prince Manman of Zheng spoke with Prince Boliao, wanting to become a high minister.

sentences.zh: 伯廖告人曰：「無德而貪，其在周易豐之離，弗過之矣。」

translations.literal: Literal: Boliao told others: "Without virtue yet greedy: in the Zhou Changes this is Feng changing to Li, and nothing surpasses it."
translations.idiomatic: Boliao told others: "Without virtue yet greedy: in the Zhou Changes this is Feng changing to Li, and nothing surpasses it."

sentences.zh: 間一歲，鄭人殺之。

translations.literal: Literal: Within a year, Zheng men killed him.
translations.idiomatic: Within a year, Zheng men killed him.

sentences.zh: 襄公二十九年，齊高子容與宋司徒見晉知伯，汝齊相禮。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xiang year twenty-nine, Gao Zirong of Qi and the Minister of Works of Song met Zhibo of Jin, with Ruqi of Qi officiating rites.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year twenty-nine, Gao Zirong of Qi and the Minister of Works of Song met Zhibo of Jin, with Ruqi of Qi officiating rites.

sentences.zh: 賓出，汝齊語知伯曰：「二子皆將不免！

translations.literal: Literal: When the guests left, Ruqi told Zhibo: "Both men will not escape disaster!
translations.idiomatic: When the guests left, Ruqi told Zhibo: "Both men will not escape disaster!

sentences.zh: 子容專，司徒侈，皆亡家之主也。

translations.literal: Literal: Zirong is overbearing and the Minister of Works is extravagant; both are lords who ruin their houses.
translations.idiomatic: Zirong is overbearing and the Minister of Works is extravagant; both are lords who ruin their houses.

sentences.zh: 專則速及，侈將以其力敝，專則人實敝之，將及矣。」

translations.literal: Literal: Overbearing men are quickly reached by disaster; the extravagant exhaust themselves by their strength, while overbearing men are in fact worn down by others. Disaster is near."
translations.idiomatic: Overbearing men are quickly reached by disaster; the extravagant exhaust themselves by their strength, while overbearing men are in fact worn down by others. Disaster is near."

sentences.zh: 九月，高子出奔燕。

translations.literal: Literal: In the ninth month, Gaozi fled to Yan.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month, Gaozi fled to Yan.

sentences.zh: 襄公三十一年正月，魯穆叔會晉歸，告孟孝伯曰：「趙孟將死矣！

translations.literal: Literal: In first month of Duke Xiang year thirty-one, Mushu of Lu returned from meeting Jin and told Meng Xiaobo: "Zhao Meng will die soon!
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Duke Xiang year thirty-one, Mushu of Lu returned from meeting Jin and told Meng Xiaobo: "Zhao Meng will die soon!

sentences.zh: 其語偷，不似民主；

translations.literal: Literal: His speech is slack and evasive, unlike that of one who governs the people;
translations.idiomatic: His speech is slack and evasive, unlike that of one who governs the people;

sentences.zh: 且年未盈五十，而諄諄焉如八九十者，弗能久矣。

translations.literal: Literal: and though not yet fifty, he speaks in the tone of one eighty or ninety; he cannot last long.
translations.idiomatic: and though not yet fifty, he speaks in the tone of one eighty or ninety; he cannot last long.

sentences.zh: 若趙孟死，為政者其韓子虖？

translations.literal: Literal: If Zhao Meng dies, will the one governing be Hanzi?
translations.idiomatic: If Zhao Meng dies, will the one governing be Hanzi?

sentences.zh: 吾子盍與季孫言之？

translations.literal: Literal: Why not speak of this with Jisun?
translations.idiomatic: Why not speak of this with Jisun?

sentences.zh: 可以樹善，君子也。」

translations.literal: Literal: One may thereby plant goodness; that is what a gentleman does."
translations.idiomatic: One may thereby plant goodness; that is what a gentleman does."

sentences.zh: 孝伯曰：「民生幾何，誰能毋偷！

translations.literal: Literal: Xiaobo said: "How long is human life? Who can avoid slackness?
translations.idiomatic: Xiaobo said: "How long is human life? Who can avoid slackness?

sentences.zh: 朝不及夕，將焉用樹！」

translations.literal: Literal: Morning may not reach evening; what use is planting?"
translations.idiomatic: Morning may not reach evening; what use is planting?"

sentences.zh: 穆叔告人曰：「孟孫將死矣！

translations.literal: Literal: Mushu told others: "Mengsun will die soon!
translations.idiomatic: Mushu told others: "Mengsun will die soon!

sentences.zh: 吾語諸趙孟之偷也，而又甚焉。」

translations.literal: Literal: I spoke to him of Zhao Meng's slackness, yet his is even worse."
translations.idiomatic: I spoke to him of Zhao Meng's slackness, yet his is even worse."

sentences.zh: 九月，孟孝伯卒。

translations.literal: Literal: In the ninth month, Meng Xiaobo died.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month, Meng Xiaobo died.

sentences.zh: 有焉。」

translations.literal: Literal: There was such a thing."
translations.idiomatic: There was such a thing."

sentences.zh: 趙孟曰：「其幾何？」

translations.literal: Literal: Zhao Meng said: "How long then?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Meng said: "How long then?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「鍼聞國無道而年穀和孰，天贊之也，鮮不五稔。」

translations.literal: Literal: He replied: "Zhen has heard that when a state lacks the Way yet harvests are harmonious and ripe, Heaven is aiding it; such aid rarely exceeds five years."
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "Zhen has heard that when a state lacks the Way yet harvests are harmonious and ripe, Heaven is aiding it; such aid rarely exceeds five years."

sentences.zh: 趙孟視蔭，曰：「朝夕不相及，誰能待五？」

translations.literal: Literal: Zhao Meng looked up to the shade and said: "Morning and evening do not reach each other; who can wait five years?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Meng looked up to the shade and said: "Morning and evening do not reach each other; who can wait five years?"

sentences.zh: 后子出而告人曰：「趙孟將死矣！

translations.literal: Literal: Houzi went out and told others: "Zhao Meng will die soon!
translations.idiomatic: Houzi went out and told others: "Zhao Meng will die soon!

sentences.zh: 主民玩歲而愒日，其與幾何？」

translations.literal: Literal: A ruler of the people toys with the year and squanders the day; how long can he have?"
translations.idiomatic: A ruler of the people toys with the year and squanders the day; how long can he have?"

sentences.zh: 冬，趙孟卒。

translations.literal: Literal: In winter, Zhao Meng died.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, Zhao Meng died.

sentences.zh: 昭五年，秦景公卒。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhao year five, Duke Jing of Qin died.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year five, Duke Jing of Qin died.

sentences.zh: 昭公十五年，晉籍談如周葬穆后，既除喪而燕，王曰：「諸侯皆有以填撫王室，晉獨無有，何也？」

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhao year fifteen, Ji Tan of Jin went to Zhou for the burial of Queen Mu. After mourning was removed, there was a banquet, and the king said: "All feudal lords have something with which to support and soothe the royal house; only Jin has nothing. Why?"
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year fifteen, Ji Tan of Jin went to Zhou for the burial of Queen Mu. After mourning was removed, there was a banquet, and the king said: "All feudal lords have something with which to support and soothe the royal house; only Jin has nothing. Why?"

sentences.zh: 籍談對曰：「諸侯之封也，皆受明器於王室，故能薦彝器。

translations.literal: Literal: Ji Tan replied: "When the feudal lords were enfeoffed, all received ritual vessels from the royal house, and therefore could present inherited vessels.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Tan replied: "When the feudal lords were enfeoffed, all received ritual vessels from the royal house, and therefore could present inherited vessels.

sentences.zh: 晉居深山，戎翟之與鄰，拜戎不暇，其何以獻器？」

translations.literal: Literal: Jin dwells deep in the mountains, neighboring Rong and Di; we are too busy bowing to the Rong, so how could we present vessels?"
translations.idiomatic: Jin dwells deep in the mountains, neighboring Rong and Di; we are too busy bowing to the Rong, so how could we present vessels?"

sentences.zh: 王曰：「叔氏其忘諸乎！

translations.literal: Literal: The king said: "Has your uncle's line forgotten?
translations.idiomatic: The king said: "Has your uncle's line forgotten?

sentences.zh: 叔父唐叔，成王之母弟，其反亡分乎？

translations.literal: Literal: Your great-uncle Tang Shu was King Cheng's younger brother; how could your share have been absent?
translations.idiomatic: Your great-uncle Tang Shu was King Cheng's younger brother; how could your share have been absent?

sentences.zh: 昔而高祖司晉之典籍，以為大正，故曰籍氏。

translations.literal: Literal: Formerly your high ancestor managed Jin's records and canons and served as Grand Corrector; therefore your clan is called Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly your high ancestor managed Jin's records and canons and served as Grand Corrector; therefore your clan is called Ji.

sentences.zh: 女，司典之後也，何故忘之？」

translations.literal: Literal: You are descendants of those in charge of the canons; why have you forgotten this?"
translations.idiomatic: You are descendants of those in charge of the canons; why have you forgotten this?"

sentences.zh: 籍談不能對。

translations.literal: Literal: Ji Tan could not answer.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Tan could not answer.

sentences.zh: 賓出，王曰：「籍父其無後乎！

translations.literal: Literal: When the guest left, the king said: "Ji Fu will likely have no descendants.
translations.idiomatic: When the guest left, the king said: "Ji Fu will likely have no descendants.

sentences.zh: 數典而忘其祖。」

translations.literal: Literal: He counts the canons yet forgets his ancestor."
translations.idiomatic: He counts the canons yet forgets his ancestor."

sentences.zh: 籍談歸，以語叔嚮。

translations.literal: Literal: Ji Tan returned and told Shu Xiang.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Tan returned and told Shu Xiang.

sentences.zh: 叔嚮曰：「王其不終乎！

translations.literal: Literal: Shu Xiang said: "Will the king fail to finish well?
translations.idiomatic: Shu Xiang said: "Will the king fail to finish well?

sentences.zh: 吾聞所樂必卒焉。

translations.literal: Literal: I have heard that what one delights in must be carried through.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that what one delights in must be carried through.

sentences.zh: 今王樂憂，若卒以憂，不可謂終。

translations.literal: Literal: Now the king delights in grief. If he ends in grief, it cannot be called a good end.
translations.idiomatic: Now the king delights in grief. If he ends in grief, it cannot be called a good end.

sentences.zh: 王一歲而有三年之喪二焉，於是乎以喪賓燕，又求彝器，樂憂甚矣。

translations.literal: Literal: Within one year, he had two three-year mournings, yet during this he used mourning guests for a banquet and also sought ritual vessels: he delights in grief too greatly.
translations.idiomatic: Within one year, he had two three-year mournings, yet during this he used mourning guests for a banquet and also sought ritual vessels: he delights in grief too greatly.

sentences.zh: 三年之喪，雖貴遂服，禮也。

translations.literal: Literal: A three-year mourning, even for the noble, is to be fully observed: this is ritual.
translations.idiomatic: A three-year mourning, even for the noble, is to be fully observed: this is ritual.

sentences.zh: 王雖弗遂，燕樂已早。

translations.literal: Literal: Even if the king did not fully observe it, making merriment in banquet was too early.
translations.idiomatic: Even if the king did not fully observe it, making merriment in banquet was too early.

sentences.zh: 禮，王之大經也；

translations.literal: Literal: Ritual is the king's great constant;
translations.idiomatic: Ritual is the king's great constant;

sentences.zh: 一動而失二禮，無大經矣。

translations.literal: Literal: one action that loses two rites means no great constant remains.
translations.idiomatic: one action that loses two rites means no great constant remains.

sentences.zh: 言以考典，典以志經。

translations.literal: Literal: Speech examines the canons; canons record the constants.
translations.idiomatic: Speech examines the canons; canons record the constants.

sentences.zh: 忘經而多言舉典，將安用之！」

translations.literal: Literal: If one forgets the constants yet speaks much and cites canons, what use are they?"
translations.idiomatic: If one forgets the constants yet speaks much and cites canons, what use are they?"

sentences.zh: 哀公十六年，孔丘卒，公誄之曰：「昊天不弔，不憖遺一老，俾屏予一人。」

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Ai year sixteen, Kong Qiu died. The duke composed a eulogy: "Vast Heaven does not pity, not even leaving one old man, but makes him shield me, this one man."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai year sixteen, Kong Qiu died. The duke composed a eulogy: "Vast Heaven does not pity, not even leaving one old man, but makes him shield me, this one man."

sentences.zh: 子贛曰：「君其不歿於魯乎？

translations.literal: Literal: Zigong said: "Will the ruler not die in Lu?
translations.idiomatic: Zigong said: "Will the ruler not die in Lu?

sentences.zh: 夫子之言曰：『禮失則昏，名失則愆。』

translations.literal: Literal: The Master's words say: "When ritual is lost, there is confusion; when names are lost, there is fault."
translations.idiomatic: The Master's words say: "When ritual is lost, there is confusion; when names are lost, there is fault."

sentences.zh: 失志為昏，失所謂愆。

translations.literal: Literal: Losing guiding purpose is confusion; losing what is proper is fault.
translations.idiomatic: Losing guiding purpose is confusion; losing what is proper is fault.

sentences.zh: 生弗能用，死而誄之，非禮也；

translations.literal: Literal: Alive, he could not employ him; dead, he eulogizes him. This is not ritual;
translations.idiomatic: Alive, he could not employ him; dead, he eulogizes him. This is not ritual;

sentences.zh: 稱『予一人』，非名也。

translations.literal: Literal: Calling him "this one man" was not proper naming.
translations.idiomatic: Calling him "this one man" was not proper naming.

sentences.zh: 君兩失之。」

translations.literal: Literal: The ruler made two errors."
translations.idiomatic: The ruler made two errors."

sentences.zh: 二十七年，公孫于邾，遂死於越。

translations.literal: Literal: In year twenty-seven, the duke fled to Zhu and eventually died in Yue.
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-seven, the duke fled to Zhu and eventually died in Yue.

sentences.zh: 庶徵之恆陽，劉向以為春秋大旱也。

translations.literal: Literal: For the regular sign of constant sunshine, Liu Xiang took it as great droughts in the Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: For the regular sign of constant sunshine, Liu Xiang took it as great droughts in the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 其夏旱雩祀，謂之大雩。

translations.literal: Literal: When summer drought required rain-prayer sacrifice, it was called the Great Yu rite.
translations.idiomatic: When summer drought required rain-prayer sacrifice, it was called the Great Yu rite.

sentences.zh: 不傷二穀，謂之不雨。

translations.literal: Literal: If it did not injure the two grain crops, it was called "no rain."
translations.idiomatic: If it did not injure the two grain crops, it was called "no rain."

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「欲德不用茲謂張，厥災荒。

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "To desire virtue yet not employ it is called stretching; its disaster is desolation.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "To desire virtue yet not employ it is called stretching; its disaster is desolation.

sentences.zh: 荒，旱也，其旱陰雲不雨，變而赤，因而除。

translations.literal: Literal: Desolation means drought; in that drought, yin clouds do not rain, then turn red and disperse.
translations.idiomatic: Desolation means drought; in that drought, yin clouds do not rain, then turn red and disperse.

sentences.zh: 師出過時茲謂廣，其旱不生。

translations.literal: Literal: Sending troops out past the proper season is called overextension; in that drought nothing grows.
translations.idiomatic: Sending troops out past the proper season is called overextension; in that drought nothing grows.

sentences.zh: 上下皆蔽茲謂隔，其旱天赤三月，時有雹殺飛禽。

translations.literal: Literal: When above and below are all obstructed, this is called separation; in that drought the sky is red for three months, and at times hail kills flying birds.
translations.idiomatic: When above and below are all obstructed, this is called separation; in that drought the sky is red for three months, and at times hail kills flying birds.

sentences.zh: 上緣求妃茲謂僭，其旱三月大溫亡雲。

translations.literal: Literal: When superiors pursue consorts through improper channels, this is called transgression; in that drought for three months there is great heat with no clouds.
translations.idiomatic: When superiors pursue consorts through improper channels, this is called transgression; in that drought for three months there is great heat with no clouds.

sentences.zh: 居高臺府，茲謂犯陰侵陽，其旱萬物根死，數有火災。

translations.literal: Literal: Dwelling in lofty towers and mansions is called violating yin and invading yang; in that drought the roots of all things die, and there are frequent fires.
translations.idiomatic: Dwelling in lofty towers and mansions is called violating yin and invading yang; in that drought the roots of all things die, and there are frequent fires.

sentences.zh: 庶位踰節茲謂僭，其旱澤物枯，為火所傷。」

translations.literal: Literal: When lower ranks exceed proper limits, this is called transgression; in that drought moist things wither and are harmed by fire."
translations.idiomatic: When lower ranks exceed proper limits, this is called transgression; in that drought moist things wither and are harmed by fire."

sentences.zh: 釐公二十一年「夏，大旱」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xi year twenty-one: "In summer, great drought."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year twenty-one: "In summer, great drought."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為齊威既死，諸侯從楚，釐尤得楚心。

translations.literal: Literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that after Duke Huan of Qi died, the feudal lords followed Chu, and Duke Xi in particular won Chu's favor.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that after Duke Huan of Qi died, the feudal lords followed Chu, and Duke Xi in particular won Chu's favor.

sentences.zh: 楚來獻捷，釋宋之執。

translations.literal: Literal: Chu came to present a victory report and released Song captives.
translations.idiomatic: Chu came to present a victory report and released Song captives.

sentences.zh: 外倚彊楚，炕陽失眾，又作南門，勞民興役。

translations.literal: Literal: Leaning outward on strong Chu, exalting yang and losing the people, and also building the south gate, he exhausted the people with labor projects.
translations.idiomatic: Leaning outward on strong Chu, exalting yang and losing the people, and also building the south gate, he exhausted the people with labor projects.

sentences.zh: 諸雩旱不雨，略皆同說。

translations.literal: Literal: For all these rain-prayer drought records, the explanations are mostly the same.
translations.idiomatic: For all these rain-prayer drought records, the explanations are mostly the same.

sentences.zh: 宣公七年「秋，大旱」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xuan year seven: "In autumn, great drought."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year seven: "In autumn, great drought."

sentences.zh: 是夏，宣與齊侯伐萊。

translations.literal: Literal: That summer, Xuan joined the marquis of Qi in attacking Lai.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, Xuan joined the marquis of Qi in attacking Lai.

sentences.zh: 襄公五年「秋，大雩」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xiang year five: "In autumn, Great Yu rain-prayer."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year five: "In autumn, Great Yu rain-prayer."

sentences.zh: 先是宋魚石奔楚，楚伐宋，取彭城以封魚石。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, Yu Shi of Song had fled to Chu; Chu attacked Song and took Pengcheng to enfeoff Yu Shi.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Yu Shi of Song had fled to Chu; Chu attacked Song and took Pengcheng to enfeoff Yu Shi.

sentences.zh: 鄭畔于中國而附楚，襄與諸侯共圍彭城，城鄭虎牢以禦楚。

translations.literal: Literal: Zheng rebelled from the central states and attached to Chu. Xiang joined the feudal lords in besieging Pengcheng and walled Hulao of Zheng to resist Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng rebelled from the central states and attached to Chu. Xiang joined the feudal lords in besieging Pengcheng and walled Hulao of Zheng to resist Chu.

sentences.zh: 是歲鄭伯使公子發來聘，使大夫會吳于善道。

translations.literal: Literal: That year the earl of Zheng sent Prince Fa on a diplomatic mission and had a grandee meet Wu at Shandao.
translations.idiomatic: That year the earl of Zheng sent Prince Fa on a diplomatic mission and had a grandee meet Wu at Shandao.

sentences.zh: 外結二國，內得鄭聘，有炕陽動眾之應。

translations.literal: Literal: Outwardly allying with two states, inwardly receiving Zheng's mission, there was the response of exalting yang and mobilizing the masses.
translations.idiomatic: Outwardly allying with two states, inwardly receiving Zheng's mission, there was the response of exalting yang and mobilizing the masses.

sentences.zh: 八年「九月，大雩」。

translations.literal: Literal: In year eight: "In the ninth month, Great Yu."
translations.idiomatic: In year eight: "In the ninth month, Great Yu."

sentences.zh: 時作三軍，季氏盛。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time the three armies were organized, and the Ji clan was powerful.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the three armies were organized, and the Ji clan was powerful.

sentences.zh: 二十八年「八月，大雩」。

translations.literal: Literal: In year twenty-eight: "In the eighth month, Great Yu."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-eight: "In the eighth month, Great Yu."

sentences.zh: 先是，比年晉使荀吳、齊使慶封來聘，是夏邾子來朝。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, year after year Jin sent Xun Wu and Qi sent Qing Feng on missions; that summer the ruler of Zhu came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, year after year Jin sent Xun Wu and Qi sent Qing Feng on missions; that summer the ruler of Zhu came to court.

sentences.zh: 襄有炕陽自大之應。

translations.literal: Literal: Xiang had the response of exalting yang and self-aggrandizement.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang had the response of exalting yang and self-aggrandizement.

sentences.zh: 昭公三年「八月，大雩」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhao year three: "In the eighth month, Great Yu."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year three: "In the eighth month, Great Yu."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為昭公即位年十九矣，猶有童心，居喪不哀，炕陽失眾。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin held that when Duke Zhao took the throne he was already nineteen, yet still had a child's mind, was not sorrowful in mourning, exalted yang, and lost the people.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that when Duke Zhao took the throne he was already nineteen, yet still had a child's mind, was not sorrowful in mourning, exalted yang, and lost the people.

sentences.zh: 六年「九月，大雩」。

translations.literal: Literal: In year six: "In the ninth month, Great Yu."
translations.idiomatic: In year six: "In the ninth month, Great Yu."

sentences.zh: 先是莒牟夷以二邑來奔，莒怒伐魯，叔弓帥師，距而敗之，昭得入晉。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, Mouyi of Ju fled over with two towns; Ju, angered, attacked Lu. Shu Gong led troops, repelled and defeated them, and Zhao was able to enter Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Mouyi of Ju fled over with two towns; Ju, angered, attacked Lu. Shu Gong led troops, repelled and defeated them, and Zhao was able to enter Jin.

sentences.zh: 外和大國，內獲二邑，取勝鄰國，有炕陽動眾之應。

translations.literal: Literal: Outwardly making peace with a great state, inwardly gaining two towns, and winning over a neighboring state had the response of exalting yang and mobilizing the masses.
translations.idiomatic: Outwardly making peace with a great state, inwardly gaining two towns, and winning over a neighboring state had the response of exalting yang and mobilizing the masses.

sentences.zh: 二十四年「八月，大雩」。

translations.literal: Literal: In year twenty-four: "In the eighth month, Great Yu."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-four: "In the eighth month, Great Yu."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為左氏傳二十三年邾師城翼，還經魯地，魯襲取邾師，獲其三大夫。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin held that in the Zuo Tradition, year twenty-three, Zhu forces walled Yi, then passed through Lu territory on return; Lu attacked and captured the Zhu troops and took their three great officers.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that in the Zuo Tradition, year twenty-three, Zhu forces walled Yi, then passed through Lu territory on return; Lu attacked and captured the Zhu troops and took their three great officers.

sentences.zh: 邾人愬于晉，晉人執我行人叔孫婼，是春乃歸之。

translations.literal: Literal: The people of Zhu appealed to Jin; Jin arrested our envoy Shusun Ruo, and that spring finally released him.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Zhu appealed to Jin; Jin arrested our envoy Shusun Ruo, and that spring finally released him.

sentences.zh: 二十五年「七月上辛大雩，季辛又雩」，旱甚也。

translations.literal: Literal: In year twenty-five: "On the first xin day of seventh month, Great Yu; on jixin day, Yu again," showing severe drought.
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-five: "On the first xin day of seventh month, Great Yu; on jixin day, Yu again," showing severe drought.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為時后氏與季氏有隙。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin held that at that time the Hou clan and Ji clan had a rift.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that at that time the Hou clan and Ji clan had a rift.

sentences.zh: 又季氏之族有淫妻為讒，使季平子與族人相惡，皆共譖平子。

translations.literal: Literal: Also, among the Ji clan was an adulterous wife who slandered, causing Ji Pingzi and kinsmen to hate one another, and all slandered Pingzi together.
translations.idiomatic: Also, among the Ji clan was an adulterous wife who slandered, causing Ji Pingzi and kinsmen to hate one another, and all slandered Pingzi together.

sentences.zh: 子家駒諫曰：「讒人以君徼幸，不可。」

translations.literal: Literal: Zijia Ju remonstrated: "Slanderers seek fortune through the ruler; this cannot stand."
translations.idiomatic: Zijia Ju remonstrated: "Slanderers seek fortune through the ruler; this cannot stand."

sentences.zh: 昭公遂伐季氏，為所敗，出奔齊。

translations.literal: Literal: Duke Zhao then attacked the Ji clan, was defeated by them, and fled to Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Zhao then attacked the Ji clan, was defeated by them, and fled to Qi.

sentences.zh: 定公十年「九月，大雩」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Ding year ten: "In the ninth month, Great Yu."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ding year ten: "In the ninth month, Great Yu."

sentences.zh: 先是定公自將侵鄭，歸而城中城。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, Duke Ding personally led an incursion into Zheng, then returned and fortified the inner city.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Duke Ding personally led an incursion into Zheng, then returned and fortified the inner city.

sentences.zh: 二大夫帥師圍鄆。

translations.literal: Literal: Two great officers led troops to besiege Yun.
translations.idiomatic: Two great officers led troops to besiege Yun.

sentences.zh: 嚴公三十一年「冬，不雨」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhuang year thirty-one: "In winter, no rain."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year thirty-one: "In winter, no rain."

sentences.zh: 是歲，一年而三築臺，奢侈不恤民。

translations.literal: Literal: That year, three terraces were built in a single year; this was extravagance without concern for the people.
translations.idiomatic: That year, three terraces were built in a single year; this was extravagance without concern for the people.

sentences.zh: 釐公二年「冬十月不雨」，三年「春正月不雨，夏四月不雨」，「六月雨」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xi year two: "In winter, tenth month, no rain"; in year three: "In spring, first month, no rain; in summer, fourth month, no rain"; "in sixth month, rain."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year two: "In winter, tenth month, no rain"; in year three: "In spring, first month, no rain; in summer, fourth month, no rain"; "in sixth month, rain."

sentences.zh: 先是者，嚴公夫人與公子慶父淫，而殺二君。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, Duke Zhuang's wife had illicit relations with Prince Qingfu and killed two rulers.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Duke Zhuang's wife had illicit relations with Prince Qingfu and killed two rulers.

sentences.zh: 國人攻之，夫人遜于邾，慶父奔莒。

translations.literal: Literal: State people attacked them; the lady withdrew to Zhu, and Qingfu fled to Ju.
translations.idiomatic: State people attacked them; the lady withdrew to Zhu, and Qingfu fled to Ju.

sentences.zh: 釐公即位，南敗邾，東敗莒，獲其大夫。

translations.literal: Literal: When Duke Xi took the throne, he defeated Zhu to the south and Ju to the east, capturing their great officers.
translations.idiomatic: When Duke Xi took the throne, he defeated Zhu to the south and Ju to the east, capturing their great officers.

sentences.zh: 有炕陽之應。

translations.literal: Literal: There was the response of exalting yang.
translations.idiomatic: There was the response of exalting yang.

sentences.zh: 十年，「自正月不雨，至于秋七月」。

translations.literal: Literal: In year ten: "From first month no rain until the seventh month of autumn."
translations.idiomatic: In year ten: "From first month no rain until the seventh month of autumn."

sentences.zh: 先是公子遂會四國而救鄭。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, Prince Sui met four states to rescue Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Prince Sui met four states to rescue Zheng.

sentences.zh: 楚使越椒來聘。

translations.literal: Literal: Chu sent Yue Jiao on a mission.
translations.idiomatic: Chu sent Yue Jiao on a mission.

sentences.zh: 秦人歸襚。

translations.literal: Literal: Qin returned mourning gifts.
translations.idiomatic: Qin returned mourning gifts.

sentences.zh: 有炕陽之應。

translations.literal: Literal: There was the response of exalting yang.
translations.idiomatic: There was the response of exalting yang.

sentences.zh: 十三年，「自正月不雨，至于秋七月」。

translations.literal: Literal: In year thirteen: "From first month no rain until the seventh month of autumn."
translations.idiomatic: In year thirteen: "From first month no rain until the seventh month of autumn."

sentences.zh: 先是曹伯、杞伯、滕子來朝，郕伯來奔，秦伯使遂來聘，季孫行父城諸及鄆。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, the earl of Cao, earl of Qi, and viscount of Teng came to court; the earl of Cheng came in flight; the earl of Qin sent Sui on a mission; and Jisun Xingfu fortified Zhu and Yun.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, the earl of Cao, earl of Qi, and viscount of Teng came to court; the earl of Cheng came in flight; the earl of Qin sent Sui on a mission; and Jisun Xingfu fortified Zhu and Yun.

sentences.zh: 二年之間，五國趨之，內城二邑。

translations.literal: Literal: Within two years, five states hastened toward Lu, while internally two towns were fortified.
translations.idiomatic: Within two years, five states hastened toward Lu, while internally two towns were fortified.

sentences.zh: 炕陽失眾。

translations.literal: Literal: This was exalting yang and losing the people.
translations.idiomatic: This was exalting yang and losing the people.

sentences.zh: 一曰，不雨而五穀皆孰，異也。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says: no rain yet the five grains all ripened, which is anomalous.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: no rain yet the five grains all ripened, which is anomalous.

sentences.zh: 文公時，大夫始顓盟會，公孫敖會晉侯，又會諸侯盟于垂隴。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Wen's time, great officers first monopolized alliances and meetings; Gongsun Ao met the marquis of Jin and again met feudal lords to covenant at Chuilong.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Wen's time, great officers first monopolized alliances and meetings; Gongsun Ao met the marquis of Jin and again met feudal lords to covenant at Chuilong.

sentences.zh: 故不雨而生者，陰不出氣而私自行，以象施不由上出，臣下作福而私自成。

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore when growth occurred despite no rain, yin did not send out qi from above but moved privately on its own, symbolizing bestowal not issued from above, while ministers below made blessings and completed things privately.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore when growth occurred despite no rain, yin did not send out qi from above but moved privately on its own, symbolizing bestowal not issued from above, while ministers below made blessings and completed things privately.

sentences.zh: 一曰，不雨近常陰之罰，君弱也。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says: no rain resembles the punishment of persistent yin; it means the ruler was weak.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: no rain resembles the punishment of persistent yin; it means the ruler was weak.

sentences.zh: 惠帝五年夏，大旱，江河水少，谿谷絕。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer of Emperor Hui year five, there was great drought: Jiang and He waters were low, and streams and valleys dried up.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Emperor Hui year five, there was great drought: Jiang and He waters were low, and streams and valleys dried up.

sentences.zh: 先是發民男女十四萬六千人城長安，是歲城乃成。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, 146,000 men and women were conscripted to wall Chang'an; that year the walling was completed.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, 146,000 men and women were conscripted to wall Chang'an; that year the walling was completed.

sentences.zh: 文帝三年秋，天下旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In autumn of Emperor Wen year three, there was drought under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Emperor Wen year three, there was drought under heaven.

sentences.zh: 是歲夏，匈奴右賢王寇侵上郡，詔丞相灌嬰發車騎士八萬五千人詣高奴，擊右賢王走出塞。

translations.literal: Literal: That summer, the Worthy King of the Right of the Xiongnu raided Shang commandery; an edict ordered Chancellor Guan Ying to dispatch 85,000 chariot and cavalry troops to Gaonu, striking him and driving him beyond the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, the Worthy King of the Right of the Xiongnu raided Shang commandery; an edict ordered Chancellor Guan Ying to dispatch 85,000 chariot and cavalry troops to Gaonu, striking him and driving him beyond the frontier.

sentences.zh: 其秋，濟北王興居反，使大將軍討之，皆伏誅。

translations.literal: Literal: That autumn, King Xingju of Jibei rebelled; the Grand General was sent to attack, and all were executed.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, King Xingju of Jibei rebelled; the Grand General was sent to attack, and all were executed.

sentences.zh: 後六年春，天下大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: Six years later, in spring, there was great drought under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Six years later, in spring, there was great drought under heaven.

sentences.zh: 先是發車騎材官屯廣昌，是歲二月復發材官屯隴西。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, chariot cavalry and trained troops were sent to garrison Guangchang; that year in second month, trained troops were again sent to garrison Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, chariot cavalry and trained troops were sent to garrison Guangchang; that year in second month, trained troops were again sent to garrison Longxi.

sentences.zh: 後匈奴大入上郡、雲中，烽火通長安，三將軍屯邊，又三將軍屯京師。

translations.literal: Literal: Later the Xiongnu greatly invaded Shang and Yunzhong commanderies; beacon fires reached Chang'an. Three generals garrisoned the frontier, and another three garrisoned the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Later the Xiongnu greatly invaded Shang and Yunzhong commanderies; beacon fires reached Chang'an. Three generals garrisoned the frontier, and another three garrisoned the capital.

sentences.zh: 景帝中三年秋，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In autumn of Emperor Jing, middle year three, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Emperor Jing, middle year three, there was great drought.

sentences.zh: 武帝元光六年夏，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer of Emperor Wu, Yuanguang year six, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Emperor Wu, Yuanguang year six, there was great drought.

sentences.zh: 是歲，四將軍征匈奴。

translations.literal: Literal: That year, four generals campaigned against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: That year, four generals campaigned against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 元朔五年春，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In spring of Yuanshuo year five, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of Yuanshuo year five, there was great drought.

sentences.zh: 是歲，六將軍眾十餘萬征匈奴。

translations.literal: Literal: That year, six generals with over one hundred thousand troops campaigned against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: That year, six generals with over one hundred thousand troops campaigned against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 元狩三年夏，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer of Yuanshou year three, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Yuanshou year three, there was great drought.

sentences.zh: 是歲發天下故吏伐棘上林，穿昆明池。

translations.literal: Literal: That year old officials throughout the realm were mobilized to cut thorns in Shanglin and dig Kunming Pool.
translations.idiomatic: That year old officials throughout the realm were mobilized to cut thorns in Shanglin and dig Kunming Pool.

sentences.zh: 天漢元年夏，大旱；

translations.literal: Literal: In summer of Tianhan year one, there was great drought;
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Tianhan year one, there was great drought;

sentences.zh: 其三年夏，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: in summer of year three, there was again great drought.
translations.idiomatic: in summer of year three, there was again great drought.

sentences.zh: 先是貳師將軍征大宛還。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, the General Ershi returned from campaign against Dayuan.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, the General Ershi returned from campaign against Dayuan.

sentences.zh: 天漢元年，發適民。

translations.literal: Literal: In Tianhan year one, registered civilians were conscripted.
translations.idiomatic: In Tianhan year one, registered civilians were conscripted.

sentences.zh: 二年夏，三將軍征匈奴，李陵沒不還。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer of year two, three generals campaigned against the Xiongnu; Li Ling was lost and did not return.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of year two, three generals campaigned against the Xiongnu; Li Ling was lost and did not return.

sentences.zh: 昭帝始元六年，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In Shiyuan year six under Emperor Zhao, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: In Shiyuan year six under Emperor Zhao, there was great drought.

sentences.zh: 先是大鴻臚田廣明征益州，暴師連年。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, Grand Herald Tian Guangming campaigned in Yizhou, keeping troops in the field for successive years.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Grand Herald Tian Guangming campaigned in Yizhou, keeping troops in the field for successive years.

sentences.zh: 宣帝本始三年夏，大旱，東西數千里。

translations.literal: Literal: In summer of Benshi year three under Emperor Xuan, there was great drought across thousands of li east and west.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of Benshi year three under Emperor Xuan, there was great drought across thousands of li east and west.

sentences.zh: 先是五將軍眾二十萬征匈奴。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, five generals with 200,000 troops campaigned against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, five generals with 200,000 troops campaigned against the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 神爵元年秋，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In autumn of Shenjue year one, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Shenjue year one, there was great drought.

sentences.zh: 是歲，後將軍趙充國征西羌。

translations.literal: Literal: That year, Rear General Zhao Chongguo campaigned against the Western Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: That year, Rear General Zhao Chongguo campaigned against the Western Qiang.

sentences.zh: 成帝永始三年、四年夏，大旱。

translations.literal: Literal: In summers of Yongshi years three and four under Emperor Cheng, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: In summers of Yongshi years three and four under Emperor Cheng, there was great drought.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳晉獻公時童謠曰：「丙之晨，龍尾伏辰，袀服振振，取虢之旂。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition records a children's rhyme in Duke Xian of Jin's time: "At dawn of bing day, Dragon Tail subdues the star; in neat red robes, they seize Guo's banner.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition records a children's rhyme in Duke Xian of Jin's time: "At dawn of bing day, Dragon Tail subdues the star; in neat red robes, they seize Guo's banner.

sentences.zh: 鶉之賁賁，天策焞焞，火中成軍，虢公其奔。」

translations.literal: Literal: Quail Fire blazes bright, Heaven's measure burns bright; when fire is at mid-sky, the army is formed, and the duke of Guo will flee."
translations.idiomatic: Quail Fire blazes bright, Heaven's measure burns bright; when fire is at mid-sky, the army is formed, and the duke of Guo will flee."

sentences.zh: 是時虢為小國，介夏陽之阨，怙虞國之助，亢衡于晉，有炕陽之節，失臣下之心。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Guo was a small state, wedged at the narrow pass of Xiayang, relying on aid from Yu, contending arrogantly with Jin, with the pattern of exalting yang and losing the hearts of subjects below.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Guo was a small state, wedged at the narrow pass of Xiayang, relying on aid from Yu, contending arrogantly with Jin, with the pattern of exalting yang and losing the hearts of subjects below.

sentences.zh: 晉獻伐之，問於卜偃曰：「吾其濟乎？」

translations.literal: Literal: When Duke Xian of Jin attacked it, he asked the diviner Bu Yan: "Will I succeed?"
translations.idiomatic: When Duke Xian of Jin attacked it, he asked the diviner Bu Yan: "Will I succeed?"

sentences.zh: 偃以童謠對曰：「克之。

translations.literal: Literal: Yan answered with the children's rhyme: "You will overcome it.
translations.idiomatic: Yan answered with the children's rhyme: "You will overcome it.

sentences.zh: 十月朔丙子旦，日在尾，月在策，鶉火中，必此時也。」

translations.literal: Literal: On the bingzi dawn of the first day of the tenth month, the sun will be in Tail, the moon in Ce, and Quail Fire at center; it must be at this time."
translations.idiomatic: On the bingzi dawn of the first day of the tenth month, the sun will be in Tail, the moon in Ce, and Quail Fire at center; it must be at this time."

sentences.zh: 冬十二月丙子朔，晉師滅虢，虢公醜奔周。

translations.literal: Literal: On the bingzi first day of the twelfth month in winter, Jin forces destroyed Guo, and Duke Chou of Guo fled to Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingzi first day of the twelfth month in winter, Jin forces destroyed Guo, and Duke Chou of Guo fled to Zhou.

sentences.zh: 周十二月，夏十月也。

translations.literal: Literal: In Zhou reckoning this was the twelfth month; in Xia reckoning, the tenth month.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhou reckoning this was the twelfth month; in Xia reckoning, the tenth month.

sentences.zh: 言天者以夏正。

translations.literal: Literal: References to Heaven use the Xia calendar.
translations.idiomatic: References to Heaven use the Xia calendar.

sentences.zh: 史記晉惠公時童謠曰：「恭太子更葬兮，後十四年，晉亦不昌，昌乃在其兄。」

translations.literal: Literal: In the Records, a children's rhyme from Duke Hui of Jin's time says: "The reverent heir is reburied; after fourteen years, Jin too will not prosper; prosperity will lie with his elder brother."
translations.idiomatic: In the Records, a children's rhyme from Duke Hui of Jin's time says: "The reverent heir is reburied; after fourteen years, Jin too will not prosper; prosperity will lie with his elder brother."

sentences.zh: 是時，惠公賴秦力得立，立而背秦，內殺二大夫，國人不說。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Duke Hui relied on Qin's power to take the throne, then betrayed Qin after enthronement, and internally killed two great officers; the people of the state were displeased.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Hui relied on Qin's power to take the throne, then betrayed Qin after enthronement, and internally killed two great officers; the people of the state were displeased.

sentences.zh: 及更葬其兄恭太子申生而不敬，故詩妖作也。

translations.literal: Literal: When he reburied his elder brother, the reverent heir Shen Sheng, without proper respect, a poetic anomaly therefore arose.
translations.idiomatic: When he reburied his elder brother, the reverent heir Shen Sheng, without proper respect, a poetic anomaly therefore arose.

sentences.zh: 後與秦戰，為秦所獲，立十四年而死。

translations.literal: Literal: Later he fought Qin and was captured by Qin, and died after fourteen years on the throne.
translations.idiomatic: Later he fought Qin and was captured by Qin, and died after fourteen years on the throne.

sentences.zh: 晉人絕之，更立其兄重耳，是為文公，遂伯諸侯。

translations.literal: Literal: The Jin people cut off his line and installed his elder brother Chong'er, who was Duke Wen, and he became hegemon over the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: The Jin people cut off his line and installed his elder brother Chong'er, who was Duke Wen, and he became hegemon over the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳文、成之世童謠曰：「鴝之鵒之，公出辱之。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition preserves a children's rhyme from Duke Wen and Duke Cheng's age: "The myna, the myna, the duke goes out and is shamed.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition preserves a children's rhyme from Duke Wen and Duke Cheng's age: "The myna, the myna, the duke goes out and is shamed.

sentences.zh: 鴝鵒之羽，公在外野，往饋之馬。

translations.literal: Literal: Myna feathers: the duke is in open fields; they bring him horses as gifts.
translations.idiomatic: Myna feathers: the duke is in open fields; they bring him horses as gifts.

sentences.zh: 鴝鵒跦跦，公在乾侯，徵褰與襦。

translations.literal: Literal: Mynas hopping: the duke is at Qianhou; they summon him with cloak and lined robe.
translations.idiomatic: Mynas hopping: the duke is at Qianhou; they summon him with cloak and lined robe.

sentences.zh: 鴝鵒之巢，遠哉搖搖，裯父喪勞，宋父以驕。

translations.literal: Literal: Myna nests, far away and swaying; Chou's father bears toil in mourning, Song's father in arrogance.
translations.idiomatic: Myna nests, far away and swaying; Chou's father bears toil in mourning, Song's father in arrogance.

sentences.zh: 鴝鵒鴝鵒，往歌來哭。」

translations.literal: Literal: Myna, myna: going they sing, coming they weep."
translations.idiomatic: Myna, myna: going they sing, coming they weep."

sentences.zh: 至昭公時，有鴝鵒來巢。

translations.literal: Literal: By Duke Zhao's time, mynas indeed came to nest.
translations.idiomatic: By Duke Zhao's time, mynas indeed came to nest.

sentences.zh: 公攻季氏，敗，出奔齊，居外野，次乾侯。

translations.literal: Literal: The duke attacked the Ji clan, was defeated, fled to Qi, and lived in the open country, stopping at Qianhou.
translations.idiomatic: The duke attacked the Ji clan, was defeated, fled to Qi, and lived in the open country, stopping at Qianhou.

sentences.zh: 八年，死于外，歸葬魯。

translations.literal: Literal: Eight years later, he died abroad and was returned for burial in Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Eight years later, he died abroad and was returned for burial in Lu.

sentences.zh: 昭公名裯。

translations.literal: Literal: Duke Zhao's personal name was Chou.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Zhao's personal name was Chou.

sentences.zh: 公子宋立，是為定公。

translations.literal: Literal: Prince Song was installed, becoming Duke Ding.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Song was installed, becoming Duke Ding.

sentences.zh: 元帝時童謠曰：「井水溢，滅灶煙，灌玉堂，流金門。」

translations.literal: Literal: A children's rhyme in Emperor Yuan's time said: "Well-water overflows, extinguishes hearth-smoke, floods Yutang, and runs through Jinmen."
translations.idiomatic: A children's rhyme in Emperor Yuan's time said: "Well-water overflows, extinguishes hearth-smoke, floods Yutang, and runs through Jinmen."

sentences.zh: 至成帝建始二年三月戊子，北宮中井泉稍上，溢出南流，象春秋時先有鴝鵒之謠，而後有來巢之驗。

translations.literal: Literal: In third month, wuzi day, of Jianshi year two under Emperor Cheng, the spring in a well in the Northern Palace gradually rose, overflowed, and flowed south, like the Spring and Autumn case where first there was a myna rhyme and later the verification of nesting.
translations.idiomatic: In third month, wuzi day, of Jianshi year two under Emperor Cheng, the spring in a well in the Northern Palace gradually rose, overflowed, and flowed south, like the Spring and Autumn case where first there was a myna rhyme and later the verification of nesting.

sentences.zh: 井水，陰也；

translations.literal: Literal: Well-water is yin;
translations.idiomatic: Well-water is yin;

sentences.zh: 灶煙，陽也；

translations.literal: Literal: hearth-smoke is yang;
translations.idiomatic: hearth-smoke is yang;

sentences.zh: 玉堂、金門，至尊之居：象陰盛而滅陽，竊有宮室之應也。

translations.literal: Literal: Yutang and Jinmen are the residence of supreme rank: it symbolized yin flourishing and extinguishing yang, a response of usurpation within the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Yutang and Jinmen are the residence of supreme rank: it symbolized yin flourishing and extinguishing yang, a response of usurpation within the palace.

sentences.zh: 王莽生於元帝初元四年，至成帝封侯，為三公輔政，因以篡位。

translations.literal: Literal: Wang Mang was born in Chuyuan year four under Emperor Yuan, was enfeoffed under Emperor Cheng, served as one of the Three Dukes assisting government, and by that means usurped the throne.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang was born in Chuyuan year four under Emperor Yuan, was enfeoffed under Emperor Cheng, served as one of the Three Dukes assisting government, and by that means usurped the throne.

sentences.zh: 成帝時童謠曰：「燕燕尾龚龚，張公子，時相見。

translations.literal: Literal: A children's rhyme in Emperor Cheng's time said: "Swallow, swallow, tail curved and fine; young lord Zhang, often seen together.
translations.idiomatic: A children's rhyme in Emperor Cheng's time said: "Swallow, swallow, tail curved and fine; young lord Zhang, often seen together.

sentences.zh: 木門倉琅根，燕飛來，啄皇孫，皇孫死，燕啄矢。」

translations.literal: Literal: At wooden gate with bronze-ring root, swallow flies in, pecks imperial grandson; imperial grandson dies, swallow pecks arrows."
translations.idiomatic: At wooden gate with bronze-ring root, swallow flies in, pecks imperial grandson; imperial grandson dies, swallow pecks arrows."

sentences.zh: 其後帝為微行出遊，常與富平侯張放俱稱富平侯家人，過河陽主作樂，見舞者趙飛燕而幸之，故曰「燕燕尾龚龚」，美好貌也。

translations.literal: Literal: Later the emperor traveled in disguise, often with Marquis Zhang Fang of Fuping, both calling themselves members of the marquis' household. Passing the residence of Princess Heyang for entertainment, he saw the dancer Zhao Feiyan and favored her; hence "swallow, swallow, tail curved and fine," meaning beautiful appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Later the emperor traveled in disguise, often with Marquis Zhang Fang of Fuping, both calling themselves members of the marquis' household. Passing the residence of Princess Heyang for entertainment, he saw the dancer Zhao Feiyan and favored her; hence "swallow, swallow, tail curved and fine," meaning beautiful appearance.

sentences.zh: 張公子謂富平侯也。

translations.literal: Literal: "Young lord Zhang" refers to the marquis of Fuping.
translations.idiomatic: "Young lord Zhang" refers to the marquis of Fuping.

sentences.zh: 「木門倉琅根」，謂宮門銅鍰，言將尊貴也。

translations.literal: Literal: "Wooden gate with bronze-ring root" refers to the bronze rings on palace gates, saying she would become exalted.
translations.idiomatic: "Wooden gate with bronze-ring root" refers to the bronze rings on palace gates, saying she would become exalted.

sentences.zh: 後遂立為皇后。

translations.literal: Literal: She was then installed as empress.
translations.idiomatic: She was then installed as empress.

sentences.zh: 弟昭儀賊害後宮皇子，卒皆伏辜，所謂「燕飛來，啄皇孫，皇孫死，燕啄矢」者也。

translations.literal: Literal: Her younger sister, the Zhao Beauty, harmed imperial sons in the inner palace; in the end all suffered punishment, which is what "swallow flies in, pecks imperial grandson; imperial grandson dies, swallow pecks arrows" meant.
translations.idiomatic: Her younger sister, the Zhao Beauty, harmed imperial sons in the inner palace; in the end all suffered punishment, which is what "swallow flies in, pecks imperial grandson; imperial grandson dies, swallow pecks arrows" meant.

sentences.zh: 成帝時歌謠又曰：「邪徑敗良田，讒口亂善人。

translations.literal: Literal: Another song in Emperor Cheng's time said: "Crooked paths ruin good fields; slanderous mouths throw good men into chaos.
translations.idiomatic: Another song in Emperor Cheng's time said: "Crooked paths ruin good fields; slanderous mouths throw good men into chaos.

sentences.zh: 桂樹華不實，黃爵巢其顛。

translations.literal: Literal: Cassia trees blossom without fruit; yellow sparrows nest on their crowns.
translations.idiomatic: Cassia trees blossom without fruit; yellow sparrows nest on their crowns.

sentences.zh: 故為人所羨，今為人所憐。」

translations.literal: Literal: Once envied by others, now pitied by others."
translations.idiomatic: Once envied by others, now pitied by others."

sentences.zh: 桂，赤色，漢家象。

translations.literal: Literal: Cassia is red in color, symbol of the Han house.
translations.idiomatic: Cassia is red in color, symbol of the Han house.

sentences.zh: 華不實，無繼嗣也。

translations.literal: Literal: Blossoms without fruit means no heir.
translations.idiomatic: Blossoms without fruit means no heir.

sentences.zh: 王莽自謂黃象，黃爵巢其顛也。

translations.literal: Literal: Wang Mang styled himself with the yellow emblem; thus "yellow sparrows nesting on its crown."
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang styled himself with the yellow emblem; thus "yellow sparrows nesting on its crown."

sentences.zh: 嚴公十七年「冬，多麋」。

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhuang year seventeen: "In winter, many elk."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year seventeen: "In winter, many elk."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為毛蟲之孽為災。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin took it as disaster from prodigies of hairy creatures.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin took it as disaster from prodigies of hairy creatures.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為麋色青，近青祥也。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took elk as green in color, a near green portent.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took elk as green in color, a near green portent.

sentences.zh: 麋之為言迷也，蓋牝獸之淫者也。

translations.literal: Literal: The word for elk suggests confusion; it is likely a licentious female beast.
translations.idiomatic: The word for elk suggests confusion; it is likely a licentious female beast.

sentences.zh: 是時，嚴公將取齊之淫女，其象先見，天戒若曰，勿取齊女，淫而迷國。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Duke Zhuang was about to take a licentious woman of Qi. The sign appeared first, Heaven warning as if saying: do not take a woman of Qi; licentiousness will confound the state.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Zhuang was about to take a licentious woman of Qi. The sign appeared first, Heaven warning as if saying: do not take a woman of Qi; licentiousness will confound the state.

sentences.zh: 嚴不寤，遂取之。

translations.literal: Literal: Zhuang did not awaken and took her anyway.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang did not awaken and took her anyway.

sentences.zh: 夫人既入，淫於二叔，終皆誅死，幾亡社稷。

translations.literal: Literal: After the lady entered, she was licentious with the two uncles; in the end all were executed, and the altars of state were nearly lost.
translations.idiomatic: After the lady entered, she was licentious with the two uncles; in the end all were executed, and the altars of state were nearly lost.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒指略同。

translations.literal: Literal: Dong Zhongshu's interpretation is roughly the same.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's interpretation is roughly the same.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「廢正作淫，大不明，國多麋。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Abandoning what is proper and practicing licentiousness, with great lack of clarity, a state has many elk."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Abandoning what is proper and practicing licentiousness, with great lack of clarity, a state has many elk."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「震遂泥，厥咎國多麋。」

translations.literal: Literal: It also says: "When thunder sinks in mud, its blame is that a state has many elk."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "When thunder sinks in mud, its blame is that a state has many elk."

sentences.zh: 昭帝時，昌邑王賀聞人聲曰「熊」，視而見大熊。

translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Zhao's time, King He of Changyi heard a human voice saying "bear"; he looked and saw a great bear.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Zhao's time, King He of Changyi heard a human voice saying "bear"; he looked and saw a great bear.

sentences.zh: 左右莫見，以問郎中令龔遂，遂曰：「熊，山野之獸，而來入宮室，王獨見之，此天戒大王，恐宮室將空，危亡象也。」

translations.literal: Literal: No attendants saw it. He asked Chamberlain Gong Sui, who said: "A bear is a mountain-wild beast, yet it comes into palace chambers, and only Your Highness sees it. This is Heaven warning Your Highness that the palace may soon be emptied, an omen of danger and ruin."
translations.idiomatic: No attendants saw it. He asked Chamberlain Gong Sui, who said: "A bear is a mountain-wild beast, yet it comes into palace chambers, and only Your Highness sees it. This is Heaven warning Your Highness that the palace may soon be emptied, an omen of danger and ruin."

sentences.zh: 賀不改寤，後卒失國。

translations.literal: Literal: He did not reform or awaken and later indeed lost his state.
translations.idiomatic: He did not reform or awaken and later indeed lost his state.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳襄公十七年十一月甲午，宋國人逐狾狗，狾狗入於華臣氏，國人從之。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition, Duke Xiang year seventeen, eleventh month, jiawu day: people of Song drove out a rabid dog; it entered Hua Chen's household and people pursued it there.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition, Duke Xiang year seventeen, eleventh month, jiawu day: people of Song drove out a rabid dog; it entered Hua Chen's household and people pursued it there.

sentences.zh: 臣懼，遂奔陳。

translations.literal: Literal: Chen became afraid and fled to Chen state.
translations.idiomatic: Chen became afraid and fled to Chen state.

sentences.zh: 先是臣兄閱為宋卿，閱卒，臣使賊殺閱家宰，遂就其妻。

translations.literal: Literal: Earlier, Chen's elder brother Yue had served as Song minister. After Yue died, Chen had ruffians kill Yue's steward and then took his wife.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, Chen's elder brother Yue had served as Song minister. After Yue died, Chen had ruffians kill Yue's steward and then took his wife.

sentences.zh: 宋平公聞之，曰：「臣不唯其宗室是暴，大亂宋國之政。」

translations.literal: Literal: Duke Ping of Song heard and said: "Chen is not only violent toward his own clan; he gravely disrupts Song's governance."
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ping of Song heard and said: "Chen is not only violent toward his own clan; he gravely disrupts Song's governance."

sentences.zh: 欲逐之。

translations.literal: Literal: He wanted to expel him.
translations.idiomatic: He wanted to expel him.

sentences.zh: 左師向戌曰：「大臣不順，國之恥也，不如蓋之。」

translations.literal: Literal: Left Master Xiang Xu said: "When a great minister is disobedient, it is the state's shame; better to cover it over."
translations.idiomatic: Left Master Xiang Xu said: "When a great minister is disobedient, it is the state's shame; better to cover it over."

sentences.zh: 公乃止。

translations.literal: Literal: The duke then stopped.
translations.idiomatic: The duke then stopped.

sentences.zh: 華臣炕暴失義，內不自安，故犬禍至，以奔亡也。

translations.literal: Literal: Hua Chen was violent and overbearing, having lost righteousness and inner peace; therefore a dog calamity came, leading to his flight and ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Hua Chen was violent and overbearing, having lost righteousness and inner peace; therefore a dog calamity came, leading to his flight and ruin.

sentences.zh: 高后八年三月，祓霸上，還過枳道，見物如倉狗，橶高后掖，忽而不見。

translations.literal: Literal: In third month of Empress Gao year eight, during purification at Bashang, on return passing Zhi Road she saw a thing like a gray dog that struck her under the arm and suddenly vanished.
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Empress Gao year eight, during purification at Bashang, on return passing Zhi Road she saw a thing like a gray dog that struck her under the arm and suddenly vanished.

sentences.zh: 卜之，趙王如意為祟。

translations.literal: Literal: Divination said Zhao King Ruyi was causing the haunting.
translations.idiomatic: Divination said Zhao King Ruyi was causing the haunting.

sentences.zh: 遂病掖傷而崩。

translations.literal: Literal: She then fell ill from the underarm wound and died.
translations.idiomatic: She then fell ill from the underarm wound and died.

sentences.zh: 先是高后鴆殺如意，支斷其母戚夫人手足，搉其服以為人彘。

translations.literal: Literal: Earlier Empress Gao had poisoned Ruyi and cut off the limbs of his mother Consort Qi, crushing her to make a "human swine."
translations.idiomatic: Earlier Empress Gao had poisoned Ruyi and cut off the limbs of his mother Consort Qi, crushing her to make a "human swine."

sentences.zh: 文帝後五年六月，齊雍城門外有狗生角。

translations.literal: Literal: In sixth month of the fifth year after Emperor Wen, outside Yong city gate of Qi there was a dog that grew horns.
translations.idiomatic: In sixth month of the fifth year after Emperor Wen, outside Yong city gate of Qi there was a dog that grew horns.

sentences.zh: 先是帝兄齊悼惠王亡後，帝分齊地，立其庶子七人皆為王。

translations.literal: Literal: Before this, after the emperor's elder brother, King Daohui of Qi, died without heir, the emperor divided Qi territory and established seven of his half-sons as kings.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, after the emperor's elder brother, King Daohui of Qi, died without heir, the emperor divided Qi territory and established seven of his half-sons as kings.

sentences.zh: 兄弟並彊，有炕陽心，故犬禍見也。
