translations.literal: Literal: The brothers were all powerful and had hearts of arrogant yang; therefore a dog calamity appeared.
translations.idiomatic: The brothers were all powerful and had hearts of arrogant yang; therefore a dog calamity appeared.

sentences.zh: 犬守御，角兵象，在前而上鄉者也。

translations.literal: Literal: Dogs symbolize guarding defense; horns symbolize arms, projecting forward and upward.
translations.idiomatic: Dogs symbolize guarding defense; horns symbolize arms, projecting forward and upward.

sentences.zh: 犬不當生角，猶諸侯不當舉兵鄉京師也。

translations.literal: Literal: A dog should not grow horns, just as feudal lords should not raise troops toward the capital.
translations.idiomatic: A dog should not grow horns, just as feudal lords should not raise troops toward the capital.

sentences.zh: 天之戒人蚤矣，諸侯不寤。

translations.literal: Literal: Heaven's warning came early, yet the feudal lords did not awaken.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning came early, yet the feudal lords did not awaken.

sentences.zh: 後六年，吳、楚畔，濟南、膠西、膠東三國應之，舉兵至齊。

translations.literal: Literal: Six years later, Wu and Chu rebelled; Jinan, Jiaoxi, and Jiaodong responded, raising troops to Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Six years later, Wu and Chu rebelled; Jinan, Jiaoxi, and Jiaodong responded, raising troops to Qi.

sentences.zh: 齊王猶與城守，三國圍之。

translations.literal: Literal: The king of Qi still held with city defense, and the three states besieged him.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Qi still held with city defense, and the three states besieged him.

sentences.zh: 會漢破吳、楚，因誅四王。

translations.literal: Literal: Han then broke Wu and Chu and accordingly executed the four kings.
translations.idiomatic: Han then broke Wu and Chu and accordingly executed the four kings.

sentences.zh: 故天狗下梁而吳、楚攻梁，狗生角於齊而三國圍齊。

translations.literal: Literal: Thus the heavenly dog descended on Liang and Wu-Chu attacked Liang; a horned dog appeared in Qi and three states besieged Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the heavenly dog descended on Liang and Wu-Chu attacked Liang; a horned dog appeared in Qi and three states besieged Qi.

sentences.zh: 漢卒破吳、楚於梁，誅四王於齊。

translations.literal: Literal: Han ultimately defeated Wu and Chu at Liang and executed four kings in Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Han ultimately defeated Wu and Chu at Liang and executed four kings in Qi.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「執政失，下將害之，厥妖狗生角。

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When those in power fail, inferiors will harm them; its portent is dogs growing horns.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When those in power fail, inferiors will harm them; its portent is dogs growing horns.

sentences.zh: 君子苟免，小人陷之，厥妖狗生角。」

translations.literal: Literal: If gentlemen merely escape and petty men trap them, its portent is dogs growing horns."
translations.idiomatic: If gentlemen merely escape and petty men trap them, its portent is dogs growing horns."

sentences.zh: 景帝三年二月，邯鄲狗與彘交。

translations.literal: Literal: In second month of Emperor Jing year three, dogs in Handan mated with pigs.
translations.idiomatic: In second month of Emperor Jing year three, dogs in Handan mated with pigs.

sentences.zh: 悖亂之氣，近犬豕之禍也。

translations.literal: Literal: This was qi of perverse disorder, a near calamity of dogs and swine.
translations.idiomatic: This was qi of perverse disorder, a near calamity of dogs and swine.

sentences.zh: 是時趙王遂悖亂，與吳、楚謀為逆，遣使匈奴求助兵，卒伏其辜。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time the king of Zhao was perversely disordered, plotting rebellion with Wu and Chu, and sent envoys to Xiongnu seeking auxiliary troops; in the end he bore his punishment.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the king of Zhao was perversely disordered, plotting rebellion with Wu and Chu, and sent envoys to Xiongnu seeking auxiliary troops; in the end he bore his punishment.

sentences.zh: 犬，兵革失眾之占；

translations.literal: Literal: Dog is an omen of military force losing the people;
translations.idiomatic: Dog is an omen of military force losing the people;

sentences.zh: 豕，北方匈奴之象。

translations.literal: Literal: swine is an image of the northern Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: swine is an image of the northern Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 逆言失聽，交於異類，以生害也。

translations.literal: Literal: Rebellious words losing hearing joined with a different kind, producing harm.
translations.idiomatic: Rebellious words losing hearing joined with a different kind, producing harm.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「夫婦不嚴，厥妖狗與豕交。

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If husband and wife are not disciplined, its portent is dogs mating with swine.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "If husband and wife are not disciplined, its portent is dogs mating with swine.

sentences.zh: 茲謂反德，國有兵革。」

translations.literal: Literal: This is called overturning virtue; the state will have warfare."
translations.idiomatic: This is called overturning virtue; the state will have warfare."

sentences.zh: 成帝河平元年，長安男子石良、劉音相與同居，有如人狀在其室中，擊之，為狗，走出。

translations.literal: Literal: In Heping year one under Emperor Cheng, in Chang'an, men Shi Liang and Liu Yin lived together. Something like a human form appeared in their room; when struck, it was a dog and ran out.
translations.idiomatic: In Heping year one under Emperor Cheng, in Chang'an, men Shi Liang and Liu Yin lived together. Something like a human form appeared in their room; when struck, it was a dog and ran out.

sentences.zh: 去後有數人被甲持兵弩至良家，良等格擊，或死或傷，皆狗也。

translations.literal: Literal: After it left, several armored figures holding weapons and crossbows came to Liang's house. Liang and others resisted and struck; some died, some were wounded, and all were dogs.
translations.idiomatic: After it left, several armored figures holding weapons and crossbows came to Liang's house. Liang and others resisted and struck; some died, some were wounded, and all were dogs.

sentences.zh: 自二月至六月乃止。

translations.literal: Literal: This lasted from second month to sixth month, then stopped.
translations.idiomatic: This lasted from second month to sixth month, then stopped.

sentences.zh: 鴻嘉中，狗與彘交。

translations.literal: Literal: During Hongjia, dogs mated with swine.
translations.idiomatic: During Hongjia, dogs mated with swine.

sentences.zh: 左氏昭公二十四年十月癸酉，王子晁以成周之寶圭湛于河，幾以獲神助。

translations.literal: Literal: In the Zuo Tradition, Duke Zhao year twenty-four, tenth month, guiyou day, Prince Chao sank the treasured gui-jade of Chengzhou into the river, hoping to gain divine aid.
translations.idiomatic: In the Zuo Tradition, Duke Zhao year twenty-four, tenth month, guiyou day, Prince Chao sank the treasured gui-jade of Chengzhou into the river, hoping to gain divine aid.

sentences.zh: 甲戌，津人得之河上，陰不佞取將賣之，則為石。

translations.literal: Literal: On jiaxu day, ferrymen found it by the river; Yin Buning took it to sell, whereupon it became stone.
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu day, ferrymen found it by the river; Yin Buning took it to sell, whereupon it became stone.

sentences.zh: 是時王子晁篡天子位，萬民不鄉，號令不從，故有玉變，近白祥也。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time Prince Chao usurped the Son of Heaven's position; the myriad people did not turn toward him and his orders were not followed, therefore there was this jade transformation, a near white portent.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Prince Chao usurped the Son of Heaven's position; the myriad people did not turn toward him and his orders were not followed, therefore there was this jade transformation, a near white portent.

sentences.zh: 癸酉入而甲戌出，神不享之驗云。

translations.literal: Literal: It entered on guiyou and came out on jiaxu, showing that the spirits did not accept it.
translations.idiomatic: It entered on guiyou and came out on jiaxu, showing that the spirits did not accept it.

sentences.zh: 玉化為石，貴將為賤也。

translations.literal: Literal: Jade turning into stone means the noble will become base.
translations.idiomatic: Jade turning into stone means the noble will become base.

sentences.zh: 後二年，子晁奔楚而死。

translations.literal: Literal: Two years later, Prince Chao fled to Chu and died.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, Prince Chao fled to Chu and died.

sentences.zh: 史記秦始皇帝三十六年，鄭客從關東來，至華陰，望見素車白馬從華山上下，知其非人，道住止而待之。

translations.literal: Literal: In the Records, year thirty-six of First Emperor of Qin, a Zheng traveler came from east of the passes; upon reaching Huayin he saw plain carriages and white horses moving up and down Mount Hua. Knowing they were not human, he stopped by the road and waited.
translations.idiomatic: In the Records, year thirty-six of First Emperor of Qin, a Zheng traveler came from east of the passes; upon reaching Huayin he saw plain carriages and white horses moving up and down Mount Hua. Knowing they were not human, he stopped by the road and waited.

sentences.zh: 遂至，持璧與客曰：「為我遺鎬池君。」

translations.literal: Literal: They arrived and gave him a jade disk, saying: "For me, deliver this to the lord of Hao Pool."
translations.idiomatic: They arrived and gave him a jade disk, saying: "For me, deliver this to the lord of Hao Pool."

sentences.zh: 因言「今年祖龍死」。

translations.literal: Literal: Then it said, "This year the Ancestral Dragon dies."
translations.idiomatic: Then it said, "This year the Ancestral Dragon dies."

sentences.zh: 忽不見。

translations.literal: Literal: It suddenly vanished.
translations.idiomatic: It suddenly vanished.

sentences.zh: 鄭客奉璧，即始皇二十八年過江所湛璧也。

translations.literal: Literal: The Zheng traveler presented the jade; it was the very disk the First Emperor had sunk in the river in year twenty-eight.
translations.idiomatic: The Zheng traveler presented the jade; it was the very disk the First Emperor had sunk in the river in year twenty-eight.

sentences.zh: 與周子晁同應。

translations.literal: Literal: It corresponds to the same pattern as Prince Chao of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: It corresponds to the same pattern as Prince Chao of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 是歲，石隕于東郡，民或刻其石曰：「始皇死而地分。」

translations.literal: Literal: That same year, a stone fell in Dong commandery, and some people carved on it: "The First Emperor dies and the land divides."
translations.idiomatic: That same year, a stone fell in Dong commandery, and some people carved on it: "The First Emperor dies and the land divides."

sentences.zh: 此皆白祥，炕陽暴虐，號令不從，孤陽獨治，群陰不附之所致也。

translations.literal: Literal: All these were white portents, caused by arrogant yang and tyrannical violence, orders not obeyed, solitary yang ruling alone, and the many yin not adhering.
translations.idiomatic: All these were white portents, caused by arrogant yang and tyrannical violence, orders not obeyed, solitary yang ruling alone, and the many yin not adhering.

sentences.zh: 一曰，石，陰類也，陰持高節，臣將危君，趙高、李斯之象也。

translations.literal: Literal: Another view says: stone is of the yin category; when yin holds high authority, ministers will endanger the ruler, as in the figures of Zhao Gao and Li Si.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says: stone is of the yin category; when yin holds high authority, ministers will endanger the ruler, as in the figures of Zhao Gao and Li Si.

sentences.zh: 始皇不畏戒自省，反夷滅其旁民，而燔燒其石。

translations.literal: Literal: The First Emperor did not fear the warning and reflect on himself; instead he exterminated nearby commoners and burned the stone.
translations.idiomatic: The First Emperor did not fear the warning and reflect on himself; instead he exterminated nearby commoners and burned the stone.

sentences.zh: 是歲始皇死，後三年而秦滅。

translations.literal: Literal: That same year the First Emperor died, and three years later Qin was destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: That same year the First Emperor died, and three years later Qin was destroyed.

sentences.zh: 孝昭元鳳三年正月，泰山萊蕪山南匈匈有數千人聲。

translations.literal: Literal: In first month of Yuanfeng year three under Emperor Zhao, south of Laiwu Mountain on Mount Tai there were rumbling sounds like the voices of several thousand people.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Yuanfeng year three under Emperor Zhao, south of Laiwu Mountain on Mount Tai there were rumbling sounds like the voices of several thousand people.

sentences.zh: 民視之，有大石自立，高丈五尺，大四十八圍，入地深八尺，三石為足。

translations.literal: Literal: People looked and found a great stone standing by itself, one zhang five chi high and forty-eight circumferences around, sunk eight chi into the ground, with three stones as its feet.
translations.idiomatic: People looked and found a great stone standing by itself, one zhang five chi high and forty-eight circumferences around, sunk eight chi into the ground, with three stones as its feet.

sentences.zh: 石立處，有白烏數千集其旁。

translations.literal: Literal: At the place where the stone stood, several thousand white crows gathered beside it.
translations.idiomatic: At the place where the stone stood, several thousand white crows gathered beside it.

sentences.zh: 眭孟以為石陰類，下民象，泰山岱宗之嶽，王者易姓告代之處，當有庶人為天子者。

translations.literal: Literal: Sui Meng held that stone belongs to yin and symbolizes common people; Mount Tai is the sacred peak where kings of changed surname announce succession, so there should be a commoner who becomes Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Sui Meng held that stone belongs to yin and symbolizes common people; Mount Tai is the sacred peak where kings of changed surname announce succession, so there should be a commoner who becomes Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 孟坐伏誅。

translations.literal: Literal: Meng was convicted and executed.
translations.idiomatic: Meng was convicted and executed.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「『

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "

sentences.zh: 復，崩來無咎。』

translations.literal: Literal: Returning collapse comes without blame."
translations.idiomatic: Returning collapse comes without blame."

sentences.zh: 自上下者為崩，厥應泰山之石顛而下，聖人受命人君虜。」

translations.literal: Literal: What falls from above is called collapse; its correspondence is the stone of Mount Tai toppling down, with a sage receiving the mandate and human rulers becoming captives.
translations.idiomatic: What falls from above is called collapse; its correspondence is the stone of Mount Tai toppling down, with a sage receiving the mandate and human rulers becoming captives.

sentences.zh: 又曰：「石立如人，庶士為天下雄。

translations.literal: Literal: It also says: "If stone stands like a person, common scholars become heroes of the realm.
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "If stone stands like a person, common scholars become heroes of the realm.

sentences.zh: 立於山，同姓；

translations.literal: Literal: If it stands on a mountain, it means same-surname rule;
translations.idiomatic: If it stands on a mountain, it means same-surname rule;

sentences.zh: 平地，異姓。

translations.literal: Literal: on flat ground, different-surname rule.
translations.idiomatic: on flat ground, different-surname rule.

sentences.zh: 立於水，聖人；

translations.literal: Literal: Standing in water means a sage;
translations.idiomatic: Standing in water means a sage;

sentences.zh: 於澤，小人。」

translations.literal: Literal: in marshland, a petty man."
translations.idiomatic: in marshland, a petty man."

sentences.zh: 天漢元年三月，天雨白毛；

translations.literal: Literal: In third month of Tianhan year one, white hair fell from heaven as rain;
translations.idiomatic: In third month of Tianhan year one, white hair fell from heaven as rain;

sentences.zh: 三年八月，天雨白氂。

translations.literal: Literal: in eighth month of year three, white fur fell from heaven as rain.
translations.idiomatic: in eighth month of year three, white fur fell from heaven as rain.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「前樂後憂，厥妖天雨羽。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Joy first and worry later: its portent is feathers raining from heaven."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Joy first and worry later: its portent is feathers raining from heaven."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「邪人進，賢人逃，天雨毛。」

translations.literal: Literal: It also says: "When wicked men advance and worthy men flee, heaven rains hair."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "When wicked men advance and worthy men flee, heaven rains hair."

sentences.zh: 史記周威烈王二十三年，九鼎震。

translations.literal: Literal: In the Records, year twenty-three of King Weilie of Zhou, the Nine Tripods shook.
translations.idiomatic: In the Records, year twenty-three of King Weilie of Zhou, the Nine Tripods shook.

sentences.zh: 金震，木動之也。

translations.literal: Literal: Metal shaking means wood moving it.
translations.idiomatic: Metal shaking means wood moving it.

sentences.zh: 是時周室衰微，刑重而虐，號令不從，以亂金氣。

translations.literal: Literal: At that time the Zhou royal house was weakened, punishments were heavy and cruel, and commands were not obeyed, thereby throwing metal qi into disorder.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the Zhou royal house was weakened, punishments were heavy and cruel, and commands were not obeyed, thereby throwing metal qi into disorder.

sentences.zh: 鼎者，宗廟之寶器也。

translations.literal: Literal: The tripods were treasured ritual vessels of the ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: The tripods were treasured ritual vessels of the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 宗廟將廢，寶鼎將遷，故震動也。

translations.literal: Literal: When the ancestral temple is about to be abandoned and the treasured tripods are about to be moved, therefore they shook.
translations.idiomatic: When the ancestral temple is about to be abandoned and the treasured tripods are about to be moved, therefore they shook.

sentences.zh: 是歲晉三卿韓、魏、趙篡晉君而分其地，威烈王命以為諸侯。

translations.literal: Literal: That year the three Jin ministers Han, Wei, and Zhao usurped the Jin ruler and divided his lands; King Weilie invested them as feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: That year the three Jin ministers Han, Wei, and Zhao usurped the Jin ruler and divided his lands; King Weilie invested them as feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 天子不恤同姓，而爵其賊臣，天下不附矣。

translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven did not care for his own royal kin but ennobled their traitorous ministers, so all under heaven no longer adhered to him.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven did not care for his own royal kin but ennobled their traitorous ministers, so all under heaven no longer adhered to him.

sentences.zh: 後三世，周致德祚於秦。

translations.literal: Literal: Three generations later, Zhou transferred mandate and fortune to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Three generations later, Zhou transferred mandate and fortune to Qin.

sentences.zh: 其後秦遂滅周，而取九鼎。

translations.literal: Literal: Afterward Qin then destroyed Zhou and seized the Nine Tripods.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Qin then destroyed Zhou and seized the Nine Tripods.

sentences.zh: 九鼎之震，木沴金，失眾甚。

translations.literal: Literal: The shaking of the Nine Tripods, wood afflicting metal, meant a profound loss of the people.
translations.idiomatic: The shaking of the Nine Tripods, wood afflicting metal, meant a profound loss of the people.

sentences.zh: 成帝元延元年正月，長安章城門門牡自亡，函谷關次門牡亦自亡。

translations.literal: Literal: In first month of Yuanyan year one under Emperor Cheng, the pivot-pin of Zhangcheng Gate in Chang'an fell off by itself; the pivot-pin of the secondary gate at Hangu Pass also fell off by itself.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Yuanyan year one under Emperor Cheng, the pivot-pin of Zhangcheng Gate in Chang'an fell off by itself; the pivot-pin of the secondary gate at Hangu Pass also fell off by itself.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「飢而不損茲謂泰，厥災水，厥咎牡亡。」

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "To be hungry yet not reduced is called excess; its disaster is flood, its fault-sign is the loss of pivot-pins."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "To be hungry yet not reduced is called excess; its disaster is flood, its fault-sign is the loss of pivot-pins."

sentences.zh: 妖辭曰：「關動牡飛，辟為亡道臣為非，厥咎亂臣謀篡。」

translations.literal: Literal: The portent text says: "When passes stir and pivots fly off, rulers follow a path of ruin and ministers commit wrongdoing; its blame-sign is rebellious ministers plotting usurpation."
translations.idiomatic: The portent text says: "When passes stir and pivots fly off, rulers follow a path of ruin and ministers commit wrongdoing; its blame-sign is rebellious ministers plotting usurpation."

sentences.zh: 故谷永對曰：「章城門通路寢之路，函谷關距山東之險，城門關守國之固，固將去焉，故牡飛也。」

translations.literal: Literal: Therefore Gu Yong replied: "Zhangcheng Gate is the route to the sleeping chambers; Hangu Pass is the strategic barrier against the lands east of the mountains. City gates and passes are the state's defenses. If defenses are departing, that is why the pivot-pins flew off."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore Gu Yong replied: "Zhangcheng Gate is the route to the sleeping chambers; Hangu Pass is the strategic barrier against the lands east of the mountains. City gates and passes are the state's defenses. If defenses are departing, that is why the pivot-pins flew off."
